posts.caption_raw:
"UIC has seven amazing cultural centers, borne from campus activism, and we are all having Open Houses next week! Come through for food, to meet us, and get to know how you can connect with us. 🔥💕✨ (flame, heart, & sparkle emoji)

First up will be the Arab American Cultural Center, Monday at noon! 

The DCC will be having ours on Monday, September 16, 12-2pm with a Zoom option 1-2pm-- please save the date and stay tuned for details next week. (Can't wait that long? Check out our events page, linked in our bio!)

***
Welcome to UIC! Come check out UIC's Centers for Cultural Understanding and Social Change (CCUSC) at UIC.

Our Fall 2024 Open Houses celebrate the activism that gave rise to each of our Centers. Meet our staff, learn about our histories and upcoming programs, and reflect on the student activism that has made an impact on UIC!

Visit diversity.uic.edu/events to learn more (link in bio).

Week 3 (next week!) 
✨ @arabamcc, Sep 9 from 12pm-1:30pm, 101 Taft Hall, arabamcc.uic.edu 
Meet our staff, Enjoy some food, Build community, Celebrate our Resilience.

✨ @uicaarcc, Sep 11 from 3pm-5pm, 723 W. Maxwell, aarcc.uic.edu
Join us for “Cookies, Karaoke, and Catalysts for Change” and learn about our services, programs, and history. Stay later for “AARCC After Dark” for more karaoke and engagement!

✨ @bccatuic, Sep 12 from 12pm-2pm, 209 Addams Hall, blacc.uic.edu 
Come visit our space, meet our staff, learn about our programming and enjoy some snacks!

Week 4 Open Houses & image descriptions in the comments! 

@uicdiversity @thisisuic".

posts.caption_clean:
"As the semester takes off, there's so many exciting things going on!! But we want to take a moment to slow down, take a beat, and honor the life of Dr. Carol Gill--scholar, disability advocate, and mentor--who passed away yesterday. Dr. Gill was a powerful voice for the existence and value of disability culture at UIC and across the world. We are so grateful for her legacy and disability ancestorship. 

From Dr. Carrie Sandahl, Interim Head of Disability and Human Development: 

"Carol was a founder of disability studies not only in our department but nationally and internationally. She was a leader in the development of disability ethics and fiercely argued for the value of lives lived with disability. Her work greatly impacted disability policy, disabled women’s health, and how we think and organize around disability identity and culture. We benefitted immeasurably from her scholarship, leadership, and friendship."

Dr. Gill started writing about and defining disability culture in the mid-1990s. She guest lectured and taught at UIC as an emeritus professor for a long while, and she's remembered for her kindness and the way she embodied disability culture values. From the day the DCC's doors opened, this quote has been on our walls in some way, shape or form: 

"The elements of our culture include, certainly, our longstanding social oppression, but also our emerging art and humor, our piecing together of our history, our evolving language and symbols, our remarkably unified worldview, beliefs and values, and our strategies for surviving and thriving." 

Dr. Carol Gill, Rest in Power

ID: Rainbow gradient with a dark center with a quotation mark symbol and the above quote in white text. 

@idhduic @uicdiversity @uicahs".

posts.caption_raw:
"Feeling settled in to the semester yet? Add some community time to the mix! We're hosting our very first Crip Coffee Break of the semester tomorrow, and it's an Icebreaker Meet & Greet. Join us! 

☕ (coffee mug emoji) Crip Coffee Break: Meet & Greet
Wednesday, September 4, 3-4pm
DCC (BSB 235) & on Zoom
Full details at the link in our bio!

Our first Crip Coffee Break will lean into the silly icebreakers (feel free to say "pass"), and we'll check in about how the start of semester's going.

Audience note: This space is a space for people with disabilities to find community--including people exploring their relationship to disability identity or people who don't have an official diagnosis. We ask that nondisabled folks respect this space and join us for our other events (psst, our Open House is coming up soon!).

This is the last event in the UIC Disability Cultural Center Dis/Orientation series, but if you're still getting acclimated, you can always ping us to meet one-on-one! 

Covid safety information:
UIC does not require masking, but we are continuing to mask indoors as an accessibility measure for sick/immunocompromised folks and those living interdependently with them. Please wear a mask!* We’ll have extras on hand.

*If masking conflicts with your access needs, please get in touch so we can work something out.

Access information:
CART (live captions) and ASL will be provided. The DCC is located on the 2nd floor and is accessible via elevator. We use fragrance free cleaning supplies in our center, but UIC is not a fragrance free campus. Please skip the cologne or perfume to make this a fragrance reduced environment!

Contact dcc@uic.edu or 312-355-7050 with any questions or requests!

[IDs in comments] 

@thisisuic  @uicdiversity  @uic.drc".

posts.caption_raw:
"Dis/Orientation is kicking off today with a panel of UIC alums!!! Come join the conversation! What's the "need to know" for UIC? The goal of this event is to share crip wisdom for navigating life at UIC. We are so excited to welcome our community members back! 

UIC Disability Cultural Center Dis/Orientation series 
Fall 2024
all events at DCC (BSB 235) & on Zoom
Full details at the link in our bio

🔥 (flame emoji) Crip Wisdom for UIC: DCC Alumni Panel
TODAY Wednesday, August 28, 3-4:30pm

Join us for a panel of UIC alums as they share their experiences as disabled students at UIC--and things they learned along the way. Some were grad students, some were undergrads (and some both!). .

Also join us for...
🛋 (couch and lamp emoji) Community Slow Down @ DCC
TOMORROW Thursday, August 29, 3-4pm

Take a break with us to slow down. We'll have our spaces set up for taking a sensory break in the midst of Week One hecticness. We'll have and optional check-in and check-out and otherwise we're going to do very, very little—together. We'll provide low lights, tea and coffee, mats and napping spots.

☕ (coffee mug emoji) Crip Coffee Break: Meet & Greet
Wednesday, September 4, 3-4pm

Get to know our community—or reconnect​! Our first Crip Coffee Break will lean into the silly icebreakers (feel free to say "pass"), and we'll check in about how the start of semester's going.

Covid safety information:
UIC does not require masking, but we are continuing to mask indoors as an accessibility measure for sick/immunocompromised folks and those living interdependently with them. Please wear a mask!* We’ll have extras on hand.

*If masking conflicts with your access needs, please get in touch so we can work something out.

Access information:
CART (live captions) and ASL will be provided. The DCC is located on the 2nd floor and is accessible via elevator. We use fragrance free cleaning supplies in our center, but UIC is not a fragrance free campus. Please skip the cologne or perfume to make this a fragrance reduced environment!

Contact dcc@uic.edu or 312-355-7050 with any questions or requests!
(IDs in the comments)

@uicorientation @uicdiversity @uic.drc @thisisuic".

Get to know our community—or reconnect! Our first Crip Coffee Break will lean into the silly icebreakers (feel free to say "pass"), and we'll check in about how the start of semester's going.

Covid safety information:
UIC does not require masking, but we are continuing to mask indoors as an accessibility measure for sick/immunocompromised folks and those living interdependently with them. Please wear a mask!* We’ll have extras on hand.

*If masking conflicts with your access needs, please get in touch so we can work something out.

Access information:
CART (live captions) and ASL will be provided. The DCC is located on the 2nd floor and is accessible via elevator. We use fragrance free cleaning supplies in our center, but UIC is not a fragrance free campus. Please skip the cologne or perfume to make this a fragrance reduced environment!

Contact dcc@uic.edu or 312-355-7050 with any questions or requests!
(IDs in the comments)

@uicorientation @uicdiversity @uic.drc @thisisuic".
Account: UIC Disability Cultural Center.

posts.caption_raw:
"New to UIC? Don't go it alone! We have some programs coming up for connecting with each other and getting the scoop on UIC. Returning students, you should come too! Meet people and share your wisdom. 

UIC Disability Cultural Center Dis/Orientation series 
Fall 2024
all events at DCC (BSB 235) & on Zoom
Full details at the link in our bio

🔥 (flame emoji) Crip Wisdom for UIC: DCC Alumni Panel
Wednesday, August 28, 3-4:30pm

Join us for a panel of UIC alums as they share their experiences as disabled students at UIC--and things they learned along the way. Some were grad students, some were undergrads (and some both!). What's the "need to know" for UIC? The goal of this event is to share crip wisdom for navigating life at UIC.

🛋 (couch and lamp emoji) Community Slow Down @ DCC
Thursday, August 29, 3-4pm

Take a break with us to slow down. We'll have our spaces set up for taking a sensory break in the midst of Week One hecticness. We'll have and optional check-in and check-out and otherwise we're going to do very, very little—together. We'll provide low lights, tea and coffee, mats and napping spots.

posts.caption_raw:
"Are you an undergraduate at UIC? Come join our team! 

We're hiring for BOTH a content creator AND a general DCC appointment. 

All applications are due by Friday August 2 on Handshake

Both positions are Federal Work Study for about 8 hours/week

Link in bio for more information! 

ID 1: A white rectangle on a teal background has golden sparkles around it and text that looks hand lettered in orange saying what is transcribed above. In the bottom right corner is a hand with cute red nail polish holding a megaphone that's emitting a teal sound blast.

ID 2: A teal background there is orange lettering that says "we're hiring" at the top, with a hand holding a megaphone that's emitting a teal sound blast in the corner. In white and yellow letters in the middle it says "content creator: social media. Undergraduate (Federal Work Study) Apply on Handshake by Aug 2! Job number 9141193. With the white DCC logo on the bottom. 

ID 3: A teal background there is orange lettering that says "we're hiring" at the top, with a hand holding a megaphone that's emitting a teal sound blast in the corner. posts.caption_raw:
"UPDATE: EVENT CANCELLED 
Dear community! We’ve had a Covid exposure, so in an abundance of caution and love for our community, we’re cancelling our Pre-Parade Sign Making Art Build and we’ll be sitting out of the parade this year. We hope everyone enjoys the time to celebrate disabled community and disabled pride together. If you were planning to march with us, you can still join the join the “open contingent” group where anyone can march!

ID: UIC DCC Pre-Parade Sign Making Art Build & Hang-out graphic with text over it that says "update: event cancelled" and "The Disability Pride Parade and the Cuerpos Justificados After Party are still happening!"

***
[[Event boost]]
Chicago Disability Pride Parade 
Saturday, July 20, starting at 11am 
departs from 400 S. Plymouth Court (under the L by the Library!)
goes down Dearborn to a festival at Daley Plaza (about 0.7 miles)

ID 2: Blue Circle with Disability Pride Parade info against a blue sky with clouds. 
(more info at the link in our bio!)
***

[[Event Boost]] 
Repost @stephaniealmamusic 
After Party: Disability Pride Will Not Be Denied 
Organized by Cuerpos Justificados
July 20, 6-10pm
HAZ Room
1706 S. Halsted
$5 suggested donation

6pm Doors Open
6:30pm DJ Kevin Cosgrove
7:30pm Open Mic: "What is Your Disability Pride?" Jamar Biggz
8:30pm Live Performance: Stephanie Alma, Tommy Carroll, Andy Slater, Kyle Werle

For access needs or inquiries, contact cuerposjustificados@gmail.com

ID: After Party Graphic with bright neon pink, aqua, blue , green, and yellow. There's an "active" international symbol of accessibility in bright blue, ochre signing hands, and logos for Cuerpos Justificados and HAZ studios. 

@uicdiversity @idhduic".

posts.caption_raw:
"Chicago Disability Pride Parade is less than a week away! 💕 (two small heart emoji)

We got a room in Harold Washington Library and we'll have posterboard and supplies. Join us to make signs & enjoy some disabled community before the parade starts! All are welcome-- UIC folks, community members, anyone.

UIC DCC Pre-Parade Sign Making Art Build & Hang-out
Saturday, July 20, starting at 9am
8th Floor Harold Washington Library (400 S. State Street)

Is it your first parade? You can march with us if you want! We'll be at the Library til about 10:45am. 

Access info: We'll have auto-captions, ASL interpreting, and the room is in an elevator building. We'll also bring extra masks-- please wear a mask to protect immunocompromised folks in our community. Questions? Other access requests? Contact dcc@uic.edu 

***
Chicago Disability Pride Parade 
Saturday, July 20, starting at 11am 
departs from 400 S. Plymouth Court (under the L by the Library!)
goes down Dearborn to a festival at Daley Plaza (about 0.7 miles)

(more info at the link in our bio!)

posts.caption_raw:
"[[virtual event boost]] San Francisco’s municipal Disability Cultural Center is here!! They’re hosting an amazing virtual launch event this evening. 🎉 (party confetti horn emoji)

time zone math for Chicago people: it kicks off at 8pm Central!

Repost from @longmoreinst
•
TONIGHT! Who’s joining us for the dance party, culture showcase and artist chat for the official virtual launch of the new SF Disability Cultural Center? Still time to register, free, with Deaf Interpreters ASL, captioning, and audio description via call in line. Disability Culture is Here! https://dcc.page/LaunchEvent

Description: The slide shows a magenta background with a colorful pattern of snails and leaves at the top. A bold title reads “Disability Culture is Here” next to the DCC logo. Smaller text reads “Join us for our launch event! A celebration of disability culture with music, film, dance, an artist panel, and a community dance party fashion show!” The details of the event read: “July 12 from 6 - 7:30pm PST. Virtual, ASL, CART. RSVP at dcc.page/LaunchEvent”. A callout says “More info” with an arrow pointing to “disabilityculturalcenter.org/calendar”.".

posts.caption_raw:
"Rest in Power, @mrcooper82. We're heartbroken to learn of the passing of Justin Cooper, advocate, community builder, videographer, and photographer. We're so grateful we got to work with you, Justin, during your residency at UIC. Your street photography is powerful and beautiful, activist and creative, everything we love about disability art (and you). We loved hearing what you had to say about your photos: how they had a lot to do with what photos are possible, and crip versions of "reading" accessibility in a scene.

We can't do all of your contributions to disability community in Chicago justice. Your impact--in terms of actual policy change, in terms of relationships, and in terms of creative work-- is something we feel deeply, and you will be sorely missed. Our streets are a little emptier without you. 

We put a few links in our bio if folks want to get to know Justin and his legacy. 

ID: "Straight Down Franklin" by Justin Cooper, a photograph of an empty street under orangey train tracks, dappled with sunlight.".

posts.caption_raw:
"Happy Holidays from the UIC Disability Resource Center. 

OFFICE CLOSED. 

The DRC will be remote on 12/18- 12/20 and will be closed on 12/24- 1/02 for Winter Break.

posts.caption_raw:
"All are welcome! UIC Accessibility Audit Stakeholder Engagement Meetings 3/13 and 3/17
 
UIC’s Office Planning, Sustainability and Project Management will be engaging with LCM Architects to undergo a massive accessibility survey of all UIC campus spaces. In preparation, they are holding two stakeholder engagement meetings for all members of the UIC community. Stakeholder feedback will help:
 
·  Identify campus accessibility goals
·  Evaluate accessibility standards
·  Rank and organize priorities
·  Establish guidelines for a full campus audit
 
Please attend one of the sessions if you are able!
 
Monday, March 13, 1 PM – 2:30 PM. Join the March 13 meeting using this direct Zoom link, https://uic.zoom.us/j/84743387874?pwd=Wnova2ZYMnJneHdQVWdHUzdwYWZ5Zz09

"Happy New Year! The Spring 2023 semester is quickly approaching and to help you get ready, the DRC will be hosting a "How to Schedule on Accommodate" office hours. These info sessions will be hosted hybrid with some in-person test scheduling office hours during the first 2-weeks of classes. The dates & times are listed below and on the attached flyer. Please make sure to bring in all your Syllabi and get ready to schedule all your tests/quizzes in one seating. 

Please also bring your laptop/tablet. All office hours are open and walk-ins welcome.

posts.caption_raw:
"UIC College of Nursing PhD student Sabrina Jamal-Eddine was selected for the 2022 Humanities Without Walls Predoctoral Career Diversity Summer Workshop.
Humanities Without Walls based at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign’s Humanities Research Institute, is a consortium of humanities centers and institutes at 16 major research universities, including UIC. It is funded by The Andrew W. Mellon Foundation.
In summer 2022, the University of Michigan’s Institute for Humanities will host the annual HWW summer workshop for doctoral students interested in learning about diverse careers as humanists.

Jamal-Eddine will be the first participant to represent the field of nursing since the fellowship program was launched in 2015 and the first applicant accepted from UIC since 2019.

Jamal-Eddine is studying the use of spoken word poetry as critical narrative pedagogy to educate nursing students about ableism and disability justice in health care. Read a profile of Jamal-Eddine to learn how her own experiences as a disabled student informed her research.
She is as a graduate student representative for the UIC Chancellor’s Committee on the Status of Persons with Disabilities. She is also creative director for the Middle Eastern North African Student Association, which focuses on health care equity for MENA populations, and the nursing graduate student senate representative. In 2019, she performed a TEDx spoken word at The University of Ohio on her experience with Islamophobia and xenophobia.

ID: UIC Nursing PhD student Sabrina Jamal-Eddine delivers her 2019 TEDx spoken word presentation at The University of Ohio on her experience with Islamophobia and xenophobia.".

posts.caption_raw:
"Talila " TL " Lewis
Abolition & Disability Justice
Thurs March 31, 3:30-5:00pm
RSVP: https://go.uic.edu/DisabilityJustice
Hosted by the @uic.dcc & the @aaccatuic, Co-sponsored by the Chicagoland Disabled People of Color Coalition and National Black Deaf Advocates, Inc. (NBDA).

Access info: ASL & CART Provided. Contact dcc@uic.edu/ 312-355-7050 with any other access requests.

Flyer description: Light blue background with a medium blue square and a photo of Talila “TL” Lewis, a Black person with short cropped hair, glasses, a white shirt, and black blazer. Bold gold text at the top says “Talila ‘TL’ Lewis,” and the access info, talk title and details are below in blue, purple, and gold text. In the lower left are logos for the UIC Disability Cultural Center and the UIC African American Cultural Center, and in the lower right is purple text with the co-sponsors, the Chicagoland Disabled People of Color Coalition and National Black Deaf Advocates.

Additional details:
Talila "TL" Lewis (@talilalewis) is a thought leader, community lawyer and organizer who connects disability justice, racial justice, and abolition.

We are looking forward to TL's presentation and thinking together about those connections as TL explains how ableism is at the heart of every form of oppression. TL will also share about how HEARD, a cross-disability advocacy organization that works with deaf/disabled incarcerated people, grew into a firmly abolitionist organization. In hosting this event, we hope to continue UIC's ongoing conversations about intersectional organizing and dreaming radically different futures.
[Read TL's full bio at https://www.talilalewis.com/about.html]".

posts.caption_raw:
"13th Annual UIC Minority Health Conference. 

Call For Abstracts. 

The 2022 UIC Minority Health Conference Planning Team would like to invite you to present your research at this year's conference on April 8th 2022 at 9am CST! Abstracts should relate to this year’s theme, Collective Trauma: The Impact of Changing Community Structure on Health Equity. Suggested topics include industry and the occupation environment, built environment, environmental Justice, healthcare practice, health and illness, mental and emotional health, community activism, race, gender and gender identity, gender-based violence, sexual orientation, prison system, religion, immigration, and COVID-19. Abstract submissions are due February 28, 2022 at 11:59pm CST via Google form. Please direct questions regarding the submission process to info.uic.mhc@gmail.com. We look forward to receiving your submission!  Image Description: White poster with red, white, black, and blue boarder that has all the information stated above. Also has a logo of the UIC Minority Health Conference.".

"Oklahoma School for the Deaf is offering their 8 week ASL Community Classes Online for free! 
Registration is open from Jan 24th - Jan 27th. 
The classes will be offered pre-recorded online.
Certificates of attendance can be obtained by completing the requirements for the course. 
For more information see the attached flyer. 
To register, click the link below! 
https://forms.gle/5uhFaAhbmovNQAUDA

Flyer ID: American Sign Language Online Classes

ASL 1 and ASL 2
Classes will upload every Monday at 6pm CST. 
Classes are free for everyone, however we ask that you consider making a donation to the OSD Foundation www.osdfoundation.net

For more information emails Oklahoma School for the Deaf at: aslclass@osd.k12.ok.us

Flyer has a white and Gray background with green and black letters. Flyer also has the school mascot, a Buffalo.".

posts.caption_raw:
"USN is here to help! We are a student initiative working under the UIC Counseling Center that focuses on building strong mental health support by providing support groups and events for the UIC community. 

Currently, we run support groups on Mondays at 11 AM or 5 PM, Tuesdays at 11 AM or 5 PM, Wednesdays at 1 PM or 2 PM, and Fridays at 12 PM. Each group would meet once a week for an hour session that may be held online/in-person. 

Instagram: @usn.uic
Website: https://www.unitedsupportnetwork.org

The link to sign up is here if you do want to advertise for us: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdc68qkhSrgkWRzkbXJHVFgQw2HnOB8_IPd-mWEQ0nQfTHXrA/viewform

ID: Image has green leaves with a soft pink silhouette of people hanging out. The background is a dark forest green. The text reads: United Support Network
Students helping Students

Join a support group for fall
Support group times:
Monday at 11am or 5pm
Tuesday at 11am or 5pm
Wednesday at 1pm or 2pm
Friday at 12pm

Because life happens.".

"USN is here to help! We are a student initiative working under the UIC Counseling Center that focuses on building strong mental health support by providing support groups and events for the UIC community. 

Currently, we run support groups on Mondays at 11 AM or 5 PM, Tuesdays at 11 AM or 5 PM, Wednesdays at 1 PM or 2 PM, and Fridays at 12 PM. Each group would meet once a week for an hour session that may be held online/in-person. 

Instagram: @usn.uic
Website: https://www.unitedsupportnetwork.org

The link to sign up is here if you do want to advertise for us: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdc68qkhSrgkWRzkbXJHVFgQw2HnOB8_IPd-mWEQ0nQfTHXrA/viewform

posts.caption_raw:
"Unlearn to Relearn:
Shifting Attitudes for Arabs with Disabilities 

November 16th from 12:00 to 1:00 pm CST
Disability? Disabled? Special Needs? What language and frameworks do/should we use in the Arab Community?

Join the Arab American Cultural Center and the Disability Cultural Center for a discussion on disability in the Arab/American Community

Featuring:

- Adnan Alaboudi, International Disability Self Advocate, Jordan
- Marina Salman, Access Living Chicago
- Randa Abdelrahim, PhD Candidate UIC

Realtime live captioning (CART) will be provided.

The Center is covered by a lighter blue sheet with writing in black and read detailing title of event and other information about the event. The logos of the two centers in red are on the bottom right and left.

For questions and/or accessibility, contact us at arabamcc@uic.edu".

posts.caption_raw:
"Calling for new members for the CCSPD on the school year 2021-2022 ! (Chancellor's Committee on the Status of Persons with Disabilities)

Deadline: Friday, April 30, 2021

The Chancellor's Committee on the Status of Persons with Disabilities (CCSPD) seeks new members for the upcoming academic year, August 2021 to July 2022. 
 
Our Mission is to promote empowerment and inclusion of disabled students, faculty, and staff at UIC. The Committee recognizes that disabled people/people with disabilities are a diverse group, including individuals with visible and non-visible disabilities. Our work is motivated by the belief that disabled people are assets to the university. 
 
•  CCSPD advises the Chancellor and other administrative units and makes recommendations on disability-related issues, policies, services, attitudes, and practices on campus.
• CCSPD provides a forum for the discussion and assessment of disability-related issues relevant to the campus and the larger community.
 
We are seeking candidates with an eagerness to promote the mission of our committee coupled with a strong desire to learn from others. All members of the UIC Community are encouraged to apply: Undergraduate Students, Graduate Students, Faculty, and Staff
 
If you are interested in serving on the CCSPD, send an email message describing:
 
• why you would like to be a part of the CCSPD
• your skills, background
• how disability-related experiences have shaped your desire to get involved
 
This is a UIC Chancellor appointment and work-approved activity. UIC faculty and staff appointed to the committee are expected to attend our monthly committee meetings. Members are also expected to participate in at least one CCSPD subcommittee. 
 
Please email your cover letter to:
Alejandro Castro (acastr1@uic.edu) and Jennifer Jackson (jmjacksn@uic.edu).
Please include "CCSPD Membership" in the subject line. Deadline: As soon as possible. 

ID: Purple to blue vertical flyer with call for membership description written on white text".

posts.caption_clean:
"Hi all, check out this event from the 2021 SPAN (The Sexualities Project at Northwestern) Online Workshop, titled "Body Fixes: Entanglements of Sexuality, Disability and Health," happening on Friday, April 16 from 9am-3 PM CST. It looks amazing!!!

RSVP: Follow the link https://bit.ly/2RcclFP and click on the flyer to register
For more information visit sexualities.northwestern.edu

The following keynote address and presentations include:

Keynote address "Queer History, Disability History, and the Politics of Health"
Regina Kunzel (Princeton University)

Additional presentations by:
"Ed Gein and the Fabrication of the 'Mad Trans Killer"
Ryan Lee Cartwright (University of California, Davis)
"In the heat of the moment: Allergic reactions through fluid exchange"
Michael Gill (Syracuse University)
"The Evidence of Trans Experience (Being Street)"
Jules Gill-Peterson (University of Pittsburgh)
"Beyond Medical Necessity: Transition Related Care and Pharmaceutical Approaches to Sexual Dissatisfaction"
Kristina Gupta (Wake Forest University)

ID: A white flyer decorated by green, blue, yellow and red multicolor gears as borders, with large dark blue title text and a purple text box containing keynote speaker details.".

posts.caption_raw:
"Do you have a disability and are unsure when and/or how to disclose it during your job search? Need advice on how to enhance your job search using Handshake? If yes, join us TOMORROW on March 16, 12-1PM CST! Presented by UIC Career Services, Disability Cultural Center, Disability Resource Center, and Disability and Human Development

RSVP at: http://go.uic.edu/disclosure

This webinar will help you:
- Get tips on how to maximize your Handshake profile, register for virtual career fairs (such as the upcoming March 18 All Majors Career Fair), and sign up for career fair sessions with recruiters.
- Learn best practices for presenting your disability identity during the job search/hiring process.
- Learn what hiring managers are looking for in job candidates
- Get advice on how to ask employers questions about their employment inclusion policies and practices

CART live captioning will be provided. For any other access needs, please contact shami3@uic.edu or (312) 996-8332.

ID: Circular cut picture of several students huddled together with writing materials on a vertical red flyer of multiple shapes and shades. On top is a red horizontal banner with event title text "Disability Disclosure During the Job Search" on yellow all-caps, and event sponsors/dates on white and blue text. Beside the picture, event details are written with white text on a light pink box with white borders.".

posts.caption_raw:
"Day 1 “Who Am I?”: We asked our athletes to share a word or phrase that represents their identity and contributes to who they are!

Being a student-athlete goes beyond the field, pool, or court. It means embracing the many facets of who we are: from swimmers and runners to first-gen students and cultural identities like ‘Latino’ and ‘Filipina.’ Each word on this board reflects a piece of us—strengthened through sport, education, and the communities we build along the way. These elements define the resilient, diverse, and ever-evolving student-athlete at UIC.

#chicagoscollegeteam #ncaainclusion #onevalley".

posts.caption_raw:
"We’re proud to highlight UIC alumnus Qusai Shikari, BS ‘08, MENGR, ‘16, during AAPI Heritage Month! As regional director of clinical technology at Kaiser Permanente, Qusai is using his deep experience to shape the future of medical tech.

A proud UIC grad with degrees in engineering and bioengineering, Qusai also volunteers on the UICAA alumni board, giving back to our community. 

Join us in celebrating his incredible contributions!

#AAPIHeritageMonth #UICAlumni #HealthcareInnovation #UICProud #UICAA".

posts.caption_raw:
"It's Research Week at Chicago's only public research university!

Celebrate the myriad ways UIC solves real-world problems through innovation and discovery at a series of events throughout the week, including UIC Impact Day, the Research and Scholarship Annual Awards and many more. See the full list of events at the link in our bio.

You can also participate in the #UICResearchMatters campaign by urging Congress to protect federal research funding at the link in our bio and by recording a 30-second video testimonial stating why research matters (video guidelines and submission form in bio).

#UICResearchWeek #ResearchWeek #ResearchMatters #UICResearch #UIC #ThisIsUIC".

posts.caption_raw:
"UIC alumna Suzet McKinney DRPH ‘09 was appointed to the University of Illinois Board of Trustees by Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker. McKinney is a true leader in the health sciences, most recently serving as principal and director of life sciences at Sterling Bay, where she led collaborations across the scientific, academic, corporate, technology and government sectors. 

She previously served as the CEO and executive director of the Illinois Medical District, overseeing a medical research hub, biotech incubator, hospitals and more than 40 health care facilities.

As McKinney puts her deep leadership experience to work in service of her new board position, she stands as a true representation of the 2025 Women’s History Month theme: Moving Forward Together: Women Educating & Inspiring Generations. 

#UICProud #WomensHistoryMonth #WomenWhoLead".

"Alumna Mili Isaza BS '23 became the role model she didn’t have. As an engineer at one of Chicago’s top engineering firms and a motivational youth speaker, Isaza shares her story of hope and resilience to inspire women and young girls to pursue STEM. 

Isaza grew up in Venezuela, where she faced hardships and viewed education as a way to improve her life. She graduated summa cum laude after 14 years of hard work and dedication. 

Her contributions to the engineering field embody the spirit of Women’s History Month. 

Click the link in our bio to read more about her story. 

#UICAA #WomenInSTEM #WomenInEngineering #CollegeOfEngineering #WomensHistoryMonth #UICAlumni #UICProud #UICExcellence #UICLeadership".

posts.caption_raw:
"Joshua Forrest, a student at UIC Law,  is using his legal education to fight for justice — advocating for prisoners’ rights, mentoring future Black lawyers and challenging a system that too often overlooks marginalized communities. A standout in his class, he excelled in moot court, earning Best Respondent and placing in the Thurgood Marshall Moot Court Competition. 

Now, through the Uptown People’s Law Center, he works on cases that defend civil rights and hold institutions accountable. Committed to changing the face of the legal profession, Forrest is ensuring that the law serves everyone — not just those it has historically favored.

#UICProud #BlackHistoryMonth #CelebrateBlackExcellence".

posts.caption_raw:
"Sixty years ago today, Chicago Mayor Richard J. Daley cut the ribbon on UIC’s Circle Campus, opening the doors to a new era of public higher education in Chicago. Built on a 100-acre site at the crossroads of Greektown and Little Italy, Circle grew rapidly — transforming from 5,000 students in its first year to 18,000 within five years. 

It was a place of opportunity then, just as it is today.

Now, with 33,906 students across 16 colleges, UIC is the largest university in one of the world’s most vibrant cities. From a commuter campus named for a freeway interchange to a leading research institution, we’ve come a long way— thanks to the tens of thousands of alumni who have walked these elevated pathways and halls, shaping UIC’s legacy.

Here’s to the past, present, and future of UIC. See how far we’ve come.

#UIC #UICAlumni #UICTraditions #UICHistory #60thAnniversary #UICCircleCampus #CircleCampus #ChicagoArchitecture".

posts.caption_raw:
"Mariam Kamal BA '15  and Abdel Hamed BS '15 met at UIC through a mutual friend during their sophomore year. 

It was love at first sight while studying in the library for finals. After meeting, they discovered they were in the same biology classes and participated in the same student organizations. 

Six years later, they got married! Now, the couple lives in Round Lake Beach with their two children, Ayza, 5, and Ayman, 2. Mariam runs her own business, Kamaltech, Inc. and Abdel works in IT at a Veterans Affairs Medical Center.

#UIC #UICProud #ValentinesDay #UICAlumni #UICLoveStory".

posts.caption_raw:
"As we celebrate Black History Month, we recognize the trailblazers shaping the future through advocacy, scholarship and policy. Marvin Slaughter BA ‘19 embodies this legacy, dedicating his career to advancing justice.

While at UIC, Slaughter co-founded the Student Advocacy Coalition, championing accessible higher education for all. Today, he continues this mission as a researcher at University of Chicago, focusing on economic stratification, intergenerational mobility and reparatory justice. 

This Black History Month, we honor changemakers like Slaughter, whose work ensures a more just future for generations to come.

Click the link in our bio to listen to Marvin Slaughter’s episode on the UIC Black Excellence Podcast. 

#BlackHistoryMonth #UIC #UICProud #UICAlumni #UICBlackLeaders #UICLeaders".

"Amanda Williams, BA '09, MEd '12, is breaking barriers and reshaping the transfer process for community college students. As executive director of student transfer success at City Colleges of Chicago, she’s expanding access to higher education — especially for underrepresented students.

Because of her leadership, the U of I System’s transfer guarantee now extends to more students nationwide, ensuring equitable opportunities. Williams' work embodies the spirit of Black History Month — uplifting communities and empowering future generations. 

Click the link in our bio to read more.

#BlackHistoryMonth #EducationEquity #StudentSuccess #UICAlumni #UIC #UICProud".

posts.caption_raw:
"UIC’s Latin American Music Festival Returns!
FREE AND OPEN TO THE PUBLIC

Join us on Saturday, October 11 at 4:00 PM for an indoor block party at Student Center East.

In conjunction with UIC Family Weekend, this family-friendly event features performances from Madera Once, Banda El Pueblo, Erendira Izguerra, and more! The lineup kicks off at 4:00 PM and closes at 10:00 PM. 🎶

RSVP through the link in our bio!

Lineup includes: Auge Huasteco Banda El Pueblo
DJ Marino
Erendira Izguerra
Lakeside Pride Latin Band
Madera Once
Mariachi Fuego

Special thanks to @pachangachicago UIC Student Engagement, and Ryze Coffee for supporting this event.

#LAMF #uic #uicflames #chicago".

posts.caption_raw:
"🔥⚡ THE COUNTDOWN IS ON… SPARK IS ONLY 3 DAYS AWAY! ⚡🔥
Get ready for the BIGGEST night of the year, UIC! 🎶💥
✅ RSVP on UIC Connection NOW!
🎟 Every student (w/ valid I-Card) can bring ONE guest → guest tickets must be purchased in advance: http://go.uic.edu/sparkpaidticket. Students + guests must check in together. Guests must present a valid ID at the door.
🚨 IMPORTANT: Doors close at 6:30PM SHARP. No exceptions. Be in line BEFORE then or you’ll miss it.
This is the night where UIC comes together—don’t just hear about it, BE about it. Let’s spark the energy! 🔥✨
#UICSPARK".

posts.caption_raw:
"✨🔥 SPARK STYLE ALERT 🔥✨
Think you’ve got the best fit for the theme? 👀 Come dressed to impress at SPARK for a chance to shine in our Best Dressed Competition!
👗 How it works:
1️⃣ SPARK staff will hand out tickets to the best themed looks.
2️⃣ Those tickets = your key to the competition.

posts.caption_raw:
"Meet CPC - Sororities in the Women's Collegiate Panhellenic Council!
The Collegiate Panhellenic Council (CPC) at UIC consists of 4 sororities: Alpha Sigma Tau, Chi Sigma Omega, Delta Phi Epsilon, and Phi Sigma Sigma. Each sorority holds its own set of values and pillars, but all come together to reach the same goal of creating a safe and welcoming space for all women. From lifelong friendships to leadership opportunities to academic support, CPC has so much to offer, not just for your undergraduate career, but for a lifetime. Formal recruitment is quickly approaching on August 31, so make sure to follow @uic.cpc and register to find your future home! 
#gocpc #panhellenic #UICFSL".

posts.caption_raw:
"The UIC Fraternity and Sorority Life (FSL) community is proud to present MEET FSL, a fun interactive signature showcase of fraternities and sororities on campus, where active members are excited to meet you!
Join us Wednesday, September 6, 11am-2pm in the Student Center East (SCE) Illinois Room. Fraternities and Sororities will have tables to share their information, and they'll be delighted to answer your questions on how to join and what makes their organization unique. Stay for the Step, Stroll, & Salute, with neat performances throughout the afternoon! We can't wait to meet all of you MEET FSL!
#UICFSL #UICLife #ThisIsUIC".

posts.caption_raw:
"As we transition to summer, the staff across all Student Engagement units are excited to welcome in all incoming first-year and transfer students for Orientation, where a leading question is, "What will you make time for?" as you consider your level of connection and engagement across campus. We're thrilled to walk with you on your journey to explore new experiences, expand your connections, and excel in your personal development! ➡️ Check out a few images of staff preparing to guide new Flames to campus, with special thanks to @uicorientation and all OLs who will truly lead the experience! 🔥

posts.caption_raw:
"UICFSL goes abroad! How did everyone enjoy their spring break? 🤩 We're thrilled to share a feature of the intersection between academics, cultural enrichment, and collective representation, with these 5 outstanding FSL student leaders showcasing their engagement and organization pride. This group was selected for a UIC study abroad program in London with the Honors College, linked to HON 124, entitled "POP! Art and Culture 1960s Britain," where students learned through the synergy of Pop Art, Fashion, and Culture in London during the 60s. Throughout the study abroad portion, students visited many museums and historical sites connected to the 60s and the Beatles. For many, it was the first time leaving the US or traveling in Europe! (Pictured L-R: Alaa Mohamed, President of Chi Sigma Omega; Natalie Malczewski, Member of Chi Sigma Omega; Felix Celestino, Vice President of Phi Kappa Psi; Emma Johnson, Member of Alpha Sigma Tau; & Berenika Lach, Member of Phi Sigma Simga).
Share your stories with us!
#UICFSL".

posts.caption_raw:
"We warmly welcome all FSL community members to join us in honoring outstanding achievements at the FSL Awards 2023, "Blossoming Through Our Memories," on Monday, April 17.
Nominations remain open thru Sunday, April 2, at go.uic.edu/fslawards2023, and at the link in our bio. 
After submitting your thoughtful nominations, use the same website to locate the Awards RSVP to secure your seat for the dinner & awards ceremony. All attendees must RSVP to enjoy the in-person ceremony on April 17 in the SCE Illinois Room!
#UICFSL
#FSLAwards2023
http://go.uic.edu/fslawards2023".

posts.caption_raw:
"Join us in CROWNING OUR ACHIEVEMENTS at the FSL Awards 2022, Tuesday, April 19, 6pm in the UIC Dorin Forum. Bring your FSL chapter and council siblings, and enjoy a night with dinner, awards, & fun, honoring the greatest achievements and excellence of character, leadership, scholarship, and service throughout the year - with awards for individuals, organizations, advisors, & alums - and all your best friends in one location!
*RSVP by April 12 to secure a seat and share dietary needs (link is in bio, too!):
http://go.uic.edu/fslawards2022
#UICFSL #CrowningOurAchievements".

posts.caption_raw:
"Take care of your greatest asset. YOU! Join the Take Care of Our Flames challenge from February 15 thru March 15, 2022. Aim for each day to be a self-care day!

FSL and SLCE are hosting a goal-setting wellness initiative to help you Reach Your Goals, this Wednesday, February 16, 12-1pm, offered in a hybrid format: in-person in SCE Room 302 -&- via Zoom. Your choice.
Be sure to Register first:
go.uic.edu/ReachYourGoals
or simply use the link in our bio.

The full month of Wellness Center programs is found at: go.uic.edu/TakeCareFlames

#TakeCareFlames #UIC #UICFSL".

posts.caption_raw:
"FSL! ❤ Participate in the UIC Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Commemoration Week 2022, January 17-21. This year's theme is "IT STARTS WITH ME: Creating a Beloved Community". Kickoff commemoration week with the UIC MLK Day of Service on January 17 (go.uic.edu/mlksignup) and participate in a variety of other events all week long, including the MLK Book Club and A Critical Conversation with Michelle Alexander, January 18. 

For details, visit: go.uic.edu/MLK2022

Tremendous thanks @uiccsi
#UICMLK2022".

posts.caption_raw:
"Juneteenth - Freedom Day: Juneteenth is the celebration and commemoration that enslaved people were free and the war had ended, while the Emancipation Proclamation had been official two and a half years prior.  Federal troops arrived in Galveston, TX, June 19, 1865, to ensure all were freed. The following year, the first of what became the annual "Jubilee Day" was organized, and celebrations have endured and evolved across communities, with gatherings, education, service, & reflection that honors its tremendous importance. 

➡️ Get engaged with @aaccatuic & Freedom Celebration events all throughout this Saturday, June 19, including the 1:00pm Screening of "Black-ish: Juneteenth"; A Day to Volunteer, supporting the Urban Growers Collective; & Virtual Cookout.

✅ Register at aacc.uic.edu

#Juneteenth #ThisIsUIC".

posts.caption_raw:
"Happy Pride Month! With much to honor and celebrate, for inclusion of all of our FSL siblings, we take time to recognize the past to ensure a future of inclusion, justice, and love. 

On June 28, 1969, a police raid at the Stonewall Inn bar in Greenwich Village, New York against LGBTQ+ people led to major action against the NYPD and their discriminatory practices towards queer people. Stormé DeLarverie resisted arrest, and Marsha P. Johnson is credited with throwing the first brick. Led by Black and Brown trans and queer folx, the rebellion lasted six days. Stonewall was an uprising and a watershed moment in LGBTQ+ history. In 1970, on the one year anniversary of Stonewall, the first ever Pride parade took place in NYC as an act of defiance, and Pride parades and celebrations, over 50 years later, have grown. There are great moments to celebrate communities and the progress made, always remembering where things have started and ensuring continued movement towards equity.

#FSLSiblings #UICFSL #LoveIsLove".

posts.caption_raw:
"ATTENTION ALL LATINX CADA STUDENTS!

LARES and CADA have collaborated to create an online space for Latinx CADA students to connect with their advisors and peers. The goal is for our Latinx CADA students to feel a sense of community and support from peers and advisors.

During these meetings you will have the opportunity to meet and learn from other CADA students, be kept up to date with LARES and CADA opportunities and more! For more information email Margarita Bautista at mbauti21@uic.edu #uiclares #uiccada".

posts.caption_raw:
"Big announcement!!!! 
UIC received the Seal of Excelencia! Excelencia in Education recognized colleges and universities across the Nation who intentionally serve Latino students and who demonstrate positive student outcomes!

The LARES program is thrilled that UIC received this award. This award is made possible by the LARES program who has paved the way for Latina/o/x to access and graduate from higher education for the past 45 yrs at UIC & the State of Illinois. And all the other units across UIC that truly care for the success of our UIC Latinx students.

LARES is blessed to have amazing advisors & strong leadership who make our program so strong and who truly care about our Latina/o/x UIC students! This award is a reminder that we will continue to be a Hispanic Serving Institution! To learn more about this award visit edexcelencia.org
#HSIProud #LARESMade".

posts.caption_raw:
"The Latin American Recruitment and Educational Services program stands in solidarity with the Black Lives Matter movement and remains deeply committed to advocating against the violence that continues to be inflicted upon Black lives in the United States. As a program that is grounded in social justice ideals, we condemn any form of racism, oppression, and violence imposed on people of color and our communities. 
As we take a moment to reflect on how we can better serve our students and communities of color, we ask all institutions of higher education to do the same. 
Black Lives DO MATTER in the United States, in Latin America, and throughout the rest of the world. The LARES program stands in solidarity and as an ally with those who continue to face systemic oppression and injustice.
We see you, we hear you, we are here for you and with you! We stand together always. 
LARES siempre esta contigo.
✊🏿✊🏽❤️".

posts.caption_raw:
"🦋 Love the LCC? Interested in getting more involved on campus in a hands-on learning style cohort? Want to join the LCC team this upcoming semester? Apply to be an LCC intern TODAY!✨🐞🐛💜🪲

🐛The Latino Cultural Center (LCC)’s Lead. Create. Change. Internship Program is designed to expand students’ academic and professional growth through project-based assignments and work-based learning with a focus on environmental and social justice. 

☀️The internship will be in-person for the Fall 2025 semester. Interns will dedicate 10 hours per week for 12 weeks with the opportunity to continue in the Spring 2026 semester. 

📚Interns can receive compensation and/or academic credit. UIC students with a Freshman - Junior academic standing preferred. Federal Work Study students are encouraged to apply. 

❓For more information reach out to LCC Associate Director, Jorge Mena Robles at jmena2@uic.edu 

🚀Visit latinocultural.uic.edu/internships to apply now!".

"In April , student organizations @mesaatuic @sjpuic @anakbayan_atuic  and @uprs.at.uic hosted this semester’s Noche de Poetas. 🌱

Our Noche de Poetas this semester was part of our Earth Month events, which centered on the theme: Another World is Happening 🎤🌎

Throughout the night, students shared their thoughts on the many discussion questions and had the opportunity to share their poems. Toward the end of the night, the students shared the community poem 📒

If you could not attend this Noche de Poetas, stay tuned for the next one in the fall semester. Be sure to visit the LCC during finals week to study and get a snack bag 📚".

posts.caption_raw:
"We kicked off our first day of our 32nd Escucha Mi Grito Conference with a Mural Tour at the @uiclcc. We got an in depth tour of the many murals and the history of the center. 🖼️

During the event the archives were available to look through. There was binders from the history of the LCC from 1976 to now, information on Rafael Cintron Ortiz who the center is named after, and even an archive of some of the work from MeSA over the years. 

If you’re ever interested in visiting the Latino Cultural Center it is a space to study and come together as community during their programs. You can also look through their many archives in their office 🗂️".

posts.caption_raw:
"As we wrap up the semester, we wanted to highlight some of our biggest programming for the Spring semester- EARTH MONTH! For our first event, we hosted a conversation between two Chicago based artists-  Delilah Salgado @artofzena and Melissa Gutierrez, creator of @nobadfruit.

Delilah and Melissa shared insights on how they merge themes of environmental and food justice within their own art practices. 

For Delilah, she shared how her upbringing in Gage Park and interest in a feminist-forward hip hop scene inspired much of the imagery she stills plays with today. More recently, she spoke on her shift in symbolism and imagery as she continues to fight for clean air in her now neighborhood, McKinley Park which is being impacted by big polluter MAT Asphalt. 

Melissa shared that growing up, she saw the way her family, the local community and farmers worked hard together to grow their food and make sure none of it went to waste. Seeing how the U.S has millions of pounds of food waste every year and yet many around the world face food insecurity, Melissa created NoBadFruit that focuses on improving the world, one bite at a time, by spreading awareness and education on food waste. 

We kept our momentum going with a film screening of the @youngcutulturalstewards documentary, “We Are Each Other’s Harvest.” After watching the film, we were joined by a panel  of Stephan Hamer (@growgreaterenglewood), Dulce Morales (@cedillofreshproduce), Viviana Moreno (La Villita Farm @lvejo20) and filmmaker Ally Almore. In the discussion, they talked about their own journeys in becoming land stewards and the challenges that arose as being BIPOC and/or LGBTQIA+. With increased cuts to funding, the panelists also touched on the importance of their organizations and how to sustain the work despite decreases in funding and resources. 

Make sure to check out and support Grow Greater Englewood, Cedillo Fresh Produce, and La Villita Farm to uplift our local land stewards! 

Thank you to @sustainableuic for funding and @uiclibrary for co-sponsoring our Earth Month program series! Make sure to join us next week for LCC Snack bags and free coffee during our regular hours 💙".

posts.caption_raw:
"Last week, we hosted our Spring semester's Noche de Poetas! 

This is the LCC's longest running public program - happening since the '90s! In true open mic night fashion, we have continued our tradition of the "community poem." 

Throughout the night, a notebook is passed around and each person is encouraged to add one line to the poem based on that night's theme. Afterwards, the poem is read aloud by the student organizations facilitating the open mic. 

Read what students and audience members wrote on the theme of "ANOTHER WORLD IS HAPPENING!" 🐛🦋🌎🌈🐝".

posts.caption_clean:
"It's Earth Month everyday here at UIC- check out this amazing @uicheritagegarden highlight! 🌻🌎🌈💫

@snleadonclimate recently proudly "...uplifted the incredible climate work of our member institutions like the @thisisuic (UIC) to inspire and encourage all of us to remain committed to fighting the climate crisis.

@uicheritagegarden is more than just a green space, it’s a movement. Rooted in cultural heritage, environmental justice, and hands-on learning, this innovative program empowers students to tackle climate challenges through urban gardening, peer education, and community partnerships. From seed libraries to monarch habitats, student-led initiatives are growing resilience, equity, and climate solutions across campus and beyond."

🦋 Check out the video here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NPNDemTftY8&t=2s

🐜 Read the article here: https://secondnature.org/2025/04/16/promoting-climate-action-through-cultural-heritage-and-environmental-justice/

🍄 To learn more about the Heritage Garden at UIC, visit https://heritagegarden.uic.edu

Video and article written by @sustainableuic.

Congrats UIC Heritage Garden! To many more fruitful seasons! Happy Earth Month 2025! 🐛🌱🐝🌎".

posts.caption_raw:
"We’re getting ready for our 2025 LCC EARTH MONTH ARTS FESTIVAL: ANOTHER WORLD IS HAPPENING !! 
 
WHEN: Wednesday, April 23rd (TOMORROW)
TIME: 2pm-4pm 
WHERE: UIC Latino Cultural Center- Lecture Center B2. Festival will be held indoors and outdoors. 

There will be lots of activities available for FREE and open to the public! One of those activities is our LCC Scavenger Hunt created by our Social Media and Marketing Team, Monse and Alenis! 

There will be 6 prizes that include: 1 terracotta pot, soil, @uicheritagegarden tea bag and seed bag, 1 button, stickers and 1 crochet caterpillar keychain!🐛

Win by being one of the first 6 people to hang in their COMPLETED bingo card! See slide two for a sneak peek of the questions! posts.caption_raw:
"Celebrate Earth Month with the UIC Latino Cultural Center! 🐛🦋

This year’s theme, “Another World is Happening,” aims to highlight the critical resistance and ground work being done by local communities amidst a dire climate crisis. Through an environmental justice (EJ) perspective, this program series serves as a way to envision and act toward a more just environmental future. We will highlight the current EJ work and organizing currently being done by our local Chicago communities. 🌈

When: Wednesday, April 23rd
Time: 2pm-4pm 
Where: UIC Latino Cultural Center-Lecture Center B2

🎉 Festival is FREE and OPEN TO ALL AUDIENCES! We hope you join us! 🎉

***Festival will take place indoors and outdoors, weather permitting. Festival will go on rain or shine. 

LCC Earth Month Arts Festival 2025: ANOTHER WORLD IS HAPPENING! 

Celebrate Earth Month with us and enjoy these free activities open to the public! 

Happening outside the LCC: 
🦋 Screenprinting with @marimachamonarcapress 
☕ Free locally roasted coffee with @anticonquistacafe 
 📸 Photo Booth (Backdrop by @magdadoesart and Props by @artistic_disasters)
📚 UIC Pop-Up Library @uiclibrary 
🌻 Chalk Art
🐛 LCC Scavenger Hunt
🌟 Button-Making

Inside the Gallery: 
Get to know these local Chicago community organizations! 
ceibacolectiva @chicago_chrp @lvejo20 @n4ejchicago @pilsen_perro @pcrchi @thefreshwaterlab @uicheritagegarden @sustainableuic 

❓Where are we located?
The Latino Cultural Center is accessible via public transit including the CTA Blue Line and bus lines including the 60, 8, among others. We are located on East campus right next to the main quad. 

This event is a part of the annual LCC Earth Month programming. This program series is funded by @sustainableuic and co-sponsored by @uiclibrary.".

posts.caption_raw:
"Join us TOMORROW, APRIL 15TH for a Conversation Between Local Artists About Environmental and Food Justice in Chicago ft. @artofzena and @nobadfruit! 

When: April 15th (TOMORROW!!)
Time: 1pm-2pm
Where: UIC Latino Cultural Center-Lecture Center B2

This program is free and open to all audiences! 💫

@artofzena is a first-generation Mexican American public artist, educator, curator, and activist based in McKinley Park, Chicago. Rooted in intersectional feminism, Chicano culture, and Underground Hip Hop, her community-based practice addresses environmental racism while using art as a tool for healing and empowerment.

Salgado currently serves as the artist advisory coordinator for the Pilsen Latina Legacies project under the Chicago Monuments Project, in collaboration with lead artist Diana Solis. She co-created the mosaic and papel picado inspired sculpture Rio de Bienvenida / River of Welcome at the Canalport Riverwalk alongside Cynthia Weiss, Citlalli Trujillo, and Freshwater Labs.

Having grown up in Gage Park and later raising a family in McKinley Park, Salgado draws deep inspiration from her experiences of disinvestment, resilience, and reconnection with nature. Her work uplifts youth and families, advocating for community-driven green space investment and the integration of nature into everyday urban life.

@nobadfruit NoBadFruit is an art brand that focuses on improving the world, one bite at a time, by spreading awareness and education on food waste. Through the approach of art, merchandise, apparel, murals and events, they strive to create a mindful, kinder, and sustainable planet. Together, we can limit the amount of hunger and teach the importance of resources that go into growing food.

This program is a part of our annual Earth Month programming series. See our previous posts for other events happening this month! 

This program is funded by the @sustainableuic and co-sponsored by @uiclibrary.".

posts.caption_raw:
"🌿🎬Join us next Thursday, April 17th for a film screening & panel discussion at @uiclcc!

🎥What: “we are each other’s harvest” documentary film screening & panel discussion 
🗺️When: Thursday, April 17th 2:00-3:00pm 
🌻Where: UIC Latino Cultural Center 
Address: 803 S. Morgan St., Lecture Center B2 (MC 218)
🎙️Featuring: Vivi Moreno @lvejo20 (Little Village Environmental Justice Organization), Stephan Hamer @heymrhemp @growgreaterenglewood (Grow Greater Englewood), Dulce Margarita Morales @cedillofreshproduce (Cedillo’s Fresh Farm), and Ally Almore @allyalmorephoto (Young Cultural Stewards digital storyteller) moderated by Nat Ayala @artistic_disasters 

“we are each other’s
harvest:
we are each other’s
business:
we are each other’s
magnitude and bond”

- Gwendolyn Brooks

“we are each other’s harvest” uplifts and celebrates Chicago’s BIPOC & LGBTQIA+ land stewards. We’ve learned so much from the bonds of kinship and cycles of care each of these humans practice with the land. Thank you all for trusting us with your stories 🌽👩🏾‍🌾🌶️👨🏿‍🌾

👩🏾‍🌾 Featured Land Stewards:

Dulce Margarita Morales (she/her/ella) at Cedillo’s Fresh Produce @cedillofreshproduce

Stephan Hamer (he/they) @heymrhemp in his grandmother’s garden @growgreaterenglewood @backyardgarden @_egmk_

Fawn Pochel (they/them) @ndn_emo_trash at First Nations Garden @firstnationsgarden

Taryn Randle (they/them) @nicole_taryn at Libations for the Ancestors Community Garden @gettinggrowncollective @parableofthegrower @growgreaterenglewood @lvejo20

Vivi Moreno (she/her/ella) at La Villita Fram @lvejo

gaby (they/them) at MicKinely Park @freedomfighterherbs @youngculturalstewards

Sergio Ruiz (he/him) at Semillas de Justicia Garden @lvejo20

bee @abeja.explorando at Marquette Park @youngculturalstewards

Moses Williams (he/they) at Stein Learning Gardens @slgstsabina

🎥Filmmaker Ally Almore @allyalmorephoto
🎬Producer Irina Zadov @irinazadov
✂️Editor Marcus Davis @latebloomz".

🎙️Featuring: Vivi Moreno @lvejo20 (Little Village Environmental Justice Organization), Stephan Hamer @heymrhemp @growgreaterenglewood (Grow Greater Englewood), Dulce Margarita Morales @cedillofreshproduce (Cedillo’s Fresh Farm), and Ally Almore @allyalmorephoto (Young Cultural Stewards digital storyteller) moderated by Nat Ayala @artistic_disasters 

“we are each other’s
harvest:
we are each other’s
business:
we are each other’s
magnitude and bond”

- Gwendolyn Brooks

“we are each other’s harvest” uplifts and celebrates Chicago’s BIPOC & LGBTQIA+ land stewards. We’ve learned so much from the bonds of kinship and cycles of care each of these humans practice with the land. Thank you all for trusting us with your stories 🌽👩🏾🌾🌶️👨🏿🌾

👩🏾🌾 Featured Land Stewards:

Dulce Margarita Morales (she/her/ella) at Cedillo’s Fresh Produce @cedillofreshproduce

Stephan Hamer (he/they) @heymrhemp in his grandmother’s garden @growgreaterenglewood @backyardgarden @_egmk_

Fawn Pochel (they/them) @ndn_emo_trash at First Nations Garden @firstnationsgarden

Taryn Randle (they/them) @nicole_taryn at Libations for the Ancestors Community Garden @gettinggrowncollective @parableofthegrower @growgreaterenglewood @lvejo20

Vivi Moreno (she/her/ella) at La Villita Fram @lvejo

gaby (they/them) at MicKinely Park @freedomfighterherbs @youngculturalstewards

Sergio Ruiz (he/him) at Semillas de Justicia Garden @lvejo20

bee @abeja.explorando at Marquette Park @youngculturalstewards

Moses Williams (he/they) at Stein Learning Gardens @slgstsabina

🎥Filmmaker Ally Almore @allyalmorephoto
🎬Producer Irina Zadov @irinazadov
✂️Editor Marcus Davis @latebloomz".

posts.caption_raw:
"Last Wednesday, we hosted our second Civic Cinema program of the semester! We screened “En San Isidro” and welcomed visiting LALS Cuban scholar Anamely Ramos Gonzalez for a post-film conversation. 🎥

The film “En San Isidro” by Katherine Bisquet followed a group of artists, cultural workers, friends, family, and other Cubans who camped inside a house in San Isidro, Cuba, in response to the unjust arrest of rapper Denis Solís, in November 2020. Solís was abducted by officers of the Cuban National Revolutionary Police Force. After his trial, he was sentenced to eight months imprisonment under alleged contempt of court. Those who barricaded themselves into the home all engaged in protest in different forms - from hunger strikes to thirst strikes. Cuban police reacted violently and often prevented food, water, and money from entering the home. In the end, the home was raided by police during the COVID-19 pandemic. 

Anamely Ramos is a Latin American and Latino Studies (LALS) Department visiting Cuban scholar. After the film, Ramos had an open discussion about her experiences as an artist in Cuba and the political state of artists and activists in Cuba. Ramos spoke from firsthand experiences as she was one of those who barricaded herself in the home in protest. After the discussion, students had the opportunity to check out books centered around the themes of Cuba and activism towards its government, provided by the @uiclibrary.

Thank you to @uiclibrary and @lalsatuic for co-sponsoring the event, and thank you to the UIC Library for allowing us to access and check out books on Cuba after the event. 📚 Don’t forget to attend our Earth Month programming starting next week! 🌎🌎".

posts.caption_raw:
"Join us for a teach in to explore responses to the present moment in higher education, a part of the Coalition for Action in Higher Education and @aaupnational's National Day of Action.

🎓 TEACH IN:
The Assault on Science, Humanities, & Human Rights: How Should We Respond Using a Justice Lens?

📅 Thursday, April 17, 2025
⏰ 3:30 - 5:30 PM
📍 UIC Forum, rooms DEF, 725 W Roosevelt
✍️ RSVP & more info at go.uic.edu/teachin

Featuring panelists:

⭐ Katherine Franke, Columbia University Law Professor (retired) and activist, a leading scholar on law, sexuality, race, and religion drawing from feminist, queer, and critical race theory

⭐ Veena Dubal, General Counsel, AAUP; Professor of Law at University of California Irvine (will join remotely)

⭐ Juan Gonzalez, award-winning journalist, Democracy Now!

⭐ Janet Lin, Professor of Emergency Medicine, UIC College of Medicine; Affiliate Professor of Community Health Sciences, School of Public Health

...plus additional local and national speakers

Hosted by the UIC Social Justice Initiative; @uicblst; Center for Urban Economic Development; Centers for Centers for Cultural Understanding and Social Change; @uicclj; @uicgws; @uicglas; @gmesuic; @irrppuic; @uichumanities; @hullhousemuseum; @lalsatuic;".

posts.caption_raw:
"With our Earth Month program starting next Tuesday, we will be highlighting the work of the artists, land stewards and community organizations that will be joining us for this program series! 💫

Our second Earth Month feature is artist @artofzena! 🎨🌻🌈

Delilah Salgado is a first-generation Mexican American public artist, educator, curator, and activist based in McKinley Park, Chicago. Rooted in intersectional feminism, Chicano culture, and Underground Hip Hop, her community-based practice addresses environmental racism while using art as a tool for healing and empowerment.

Salgado currently serves as the artist advisory coordinator for the Pilsen Latina Legacies project under the Chicago Monuments Project, in collaboration with lead artist Diana Solis. She co-created the mosaic and papel picado inspired sculpture Rio de Bienvenida / River of Welcome at the Canalport Riverwalk alongside Cynthia Weiss, Citlalli Trujillo, and Freshwater Labs.

Having grown up in Gage Park and later raising a family in McKinley Park, Salgado draws deep inspiration from her experiences of disinvestment, resilience, and reconnection with nature. Her work uplifts youth and families, advocating for community-driven green space investment and the integration of nature into everyday urban life.

Delilah will be joining us for a conversation on Environmental and Food Justice in Chicago alongside artist @nobadfruit! 🥝

When: Tuesday, April 15th
Time: 1pm-2pm
Where: UIC Latino Cultural Center, Lecture Center B2

All programs are free and open to all audiences! We hope to see you there! 🎨

Stay tuned to learn about our other featured artists, land stewards and community organizations that will be joining us this Earth Month!

LCC Earth Month program series is funded by the UIC Sustainability Fund @sustainableuic and co-sponsored by @uiclibrary.".

posts.caption_raw:
"With our Earth Month program starting next Tuesday, we will be highlighting the work of the artists, land stewards and community organizations that will be joining us for this program series! 💫

Our first Earth Month feature is artist and creator of @nobadfruit Melissa Gutierrez! 🍓🍏🥝

@nobadfruit is an art brand that focuses on improving the world, one bite at a time, by spreading awareness and education on food waste. Through the approach of art, merchandise, apparel, murals and events, they strive to create a mindful, kinder, and sustainable planet. Together, we can limit the amount of hunger and teach the importance of resources that go into growing food. 🐛

Melissa will be joining us for a conversation on Environmental and Food Justice in Chicago alongside artist @artofzena! 

When: Tuesday, April 15th
Time: 1pm-2pm
Where: UIC Latino Cultural Center, Lecture Center B2

All programs are free and open to all audiences! We hope to see you there! 🍋

Stay tuned to learn about our other featured artists, land stewards and community organizations that will be joining us this Earth Month!

LCC Earth Month program series is funded by the UIC Sustainability Fund @sustainableuic and co-sponsored by @uiclibrary.".

posts.caption_raw:
"It's that time of year again! 🎉

Join the UIC Latino Cultural Center in honoring Earth Month via our engaging public program series! 🐛

🦋 This year’s theme, “Another World is Happening,” aims to highlight the critical resistance and ground work being done by local communities amidst a dire climate crisis!

All events programs are free and open to all audiences at the UIC Latino Cultural Center-Lecture Center B2. 🎉

🎨 A Conversation between local Artists about Environmental and Food Justice in Chicago ft. @artofzena and @nobadfruit 
When: Tuesday, April 15th, 2025
Time: 1pm-2pm

📽️ we are each other's harvest Film Screening and Panel Conversation @youngculturalstewards 
When: Thursday, April 17th, 2025
Time: 2pm-3pm ***UPDATED TIME***

🌎 Earth Month Arts Festival: Another World is Happening ft. @anticonquistacafe 
When: Wednesday, April 23rd, 2025
Time: 2pm-4pm

Celebrate Earth Month with the Latino Cultural Center at our interactive arts festival. There will be chalk art, button-making, screen printing and more! You will also have the opportunity to chat with local ej orgs such as @thefreshwaterlab @pcrchi @lvejo20 @n4ejchicago @pilsen_perro @chicago_chrp @ceibacolectiva @sustainableuic @uicheritagegarden and more!

🎤 Noche de Poetas (Student Open Mic Night) ft. @mesaatuic @uprs.at.uic @anakbayan_atuic @sjpuic 
When: Thursday, April 24th, 2025
Time: 5:30pm-7pm

This program series is funded by the UIC Sustainability Fund and co-sponsored by the University Library. 

Make sure to stay tuned for further details on our programs!".

posts.caption_raw: "In case you missed our screening of Jamaica y Tamarindo: Afro Tradition in the Heart of Mexico a few weeks ago, we had the UIC Library bring a pop-up collection of curated books centered around the theme of Afro-Latinidad. If you didn’t have the chance to check out any books but are still interested, we have provided a short list of our favorite picks and additional books to check out! Thank you to @uiclibrary for pulling these books out for us and providing us with their mobile library! 📚’".

posts.caption_raw:
"Join @sanctuaryforalluic's ART STUDIO: FIGHT FOR HIGHER EDUCATION! 🎨🎨🎨

Public education is under attack as historic advancements are rolled back at all levels. Together, we can push back against these attacks to protect worker autonomy, education as a civil right, and the freedom to teach and learn.

Join us for a drop-in art studio as we get creative, make posters and prepare for the People's University: April 17th National Day of Action for Higher Education at UIC!

When: Thursday, April 10th
Where: UIC Latino Cultural Center-Lecture Center B2
Time: 12pm-2pm

Lunch and art materials provided! Lunch is first come, first-served. 

This program is co-sponsored by the Department of Criminology, Law and Justice (CLJ).".

posts.text_for_embedding:
"Join @sanctuaryforalluic's ART STUDIO: FIGHT FOR HIGHER EDUCATION! 🎨

Public education is under attack as historic advancements are rolled back at all levels. Together, we can push back against these attacks to protect worker autonomy, education as a civil right, and the freedom to teach and learn.

Join us for a drop-in art studio as we get creative, make posters and prepare for the People's University: April 17th National Day of Action for Higher Education at UIC!

When: Thursday, April 10th
Where: UIC Latino Cultural Center-Lecture Center B2
Time: 12pm-2pm

Lunch and art materials provided! Lunch is first come, first-served. 

This program is co-sponsored by the Department of Criminology, Law and Justice (CLJ).".
Account: UIC Latino Cultural Center.

We want to thank our Sin Binarios for putting all of this together,  all of our members who helped set up and take down all the stuff on Monday, to the @uiclcc for allowing us to use the space and @uicgsc  for allowing us to use the space as a drop off location.Lastly, thank you to everyone who came by and shopped to help raise funds for trans Chicago. We raised over $400 ❤️‍🔥

Make sure to stay updated for any future MeSA events!".

posts.caption_raw:
"Last Wednesday, we hosted our first Civic Cinema of the semester! We screened the short film Jamaica & Tamarindo: Afro Tradition in the Heart of Mexico and enjoyed fresh aguas frescas! The film centered around the lives of five Afro-Mexicanos and how they navigated their identities while growing up through challenges of racism, isolatedness, and unfamiliarity with their intersecting heritages. Through the exploration of jamaica, a popular flower used for culinary, textile, and medicinal use, and tamarind, a leguminous fruit often used in spices, they were able to connect to their African roots with the plants’ prominence and importance in Mexican culture. 🌺

Students were able to enjoy the film and stay for a post-film discussion moderated by Diosmery Roque. Disomery discussed her own journey discovering her Afro-Mexican roots later in life and how that became a crucial part of her identity. Additionally, Diosmery brought her own personal collection of Afro-latinidad books for students to look through. During the Q&A, Disomery discussed how her upbringing and lack of representation of African heritage affected her sense of belonging in her communities.

Students were able to check out the @uiclibrary Pop-Up Library during the event. The library brought a variety of different genres of books ranging from fantasy, personal memoirs, fiction, and historical books connecting to Afro- Latinidad. Students were able to check out books directly at the event! 📚

Thank you so much to Diosmery @dor_doom for facilitating our post-screening conversation. Also thank you to the @bccatuic and @sdsatuic for co-sponsoring this event and to @uiclibrary for bringing their pop-up library to our space!

Make sure to join us for our next Civic Cinema on Wednesday, April 2nd from 1pm-2pm where we will be watching En San Isidro and discussing afterwards! ✨".

posts.caption_raw:
"❗❗❗ Join us for our last virtual Zona Abierta of the semester ft. Dr.Kumar Ramanathan from the Department of Political Science!

❗Reviving Local Democracy: 
New Conflicts in the Chicago City Council 
 
Presented by the UIC Latino Cultural Center 
 
WHEN: Wednesday, April 16, 2025 | 2-3pm CST 

WHERE: Virtually on Zoom 
RSVP: https://go.uic.edu/ZA416

Join us as UIC postdoctoral scholars share their experiences and respective research with the UIC community. Through an intersectional lens, these scholars reveal the complexity and specificity of their contemporary research and how the lives of those in the margins are impacted. 

Dr. Kumar Ramanathan, from the Department of Political Science, will present ongoing research on the politics of the contemporary Chicago City Council. His research shows how conflicts over inequality have transformed the Council—after decades where alderpeople were content to let the mayor control policy making, they are now asserting their role as legislators. He will discuss the new dividing lines within the Council and what they mean for the future of Chicago politics.

For access needs or questions, email lcc@uic.edu or call 312-996-3095. Auto-captions enabled on Zoom. ASL Interpretation services may be available by contacting the LCC 5 business days before the program. All audiences are welcome. 

Make sure to RSVP! Program is free and open to all audiences!".

posts.caption_raw:
"❗️🎞️🎥CIVIC CINEMA - SPRING 2025 🎥 🎞️ ❗️

“Inside San Isidro” by Katherine Bisquet

All audiences are welcome. Run Time: 28 mins. 

When: Wednesday, April 2nd, 2025
Where: Latino Cultural Center-Lecture Center B2
Time: 1pm-2pm

Join us for a short film and conversation surrounding the 2020 San Isidro artist protests in Cuba. In November 2020, rapper Denis Solís was kidnapped by officers of the Cuban National Revolutionary Police Force and sentenced to eight months of imprisonment. In protest, a group of activists barricaded themselves inside the headquarters of the San Isidro organization to demand freedom for Solís and all Cubans. 

Post-screening conversation facilitated by LALS Visiting Cuban Scholar Anamely Ramos González.

Post-film conversation is in Spanish with English subtitles. Conversation will be held primarily in Spanish, with English interpretation available. 

We will once again be joined by the UIC Pop-Up Library! After the program, browse a selection of titles related to the themes of the film courtesy of the UIC Library! UIC students, staff and faculty are able to check-out books during the event. Books will need to be need to be returned to the UIC University library. 📚 

This program is co-sponsored by @lalsatuic and @uiclibrary.

We hope to see you there! 🎉🎥🎞️".

posts.caption_raw:
"🐛🔥🎵Join us this upcoming Thursday (3/20) for our fourth Listening Party of the semester! 🐛🔥🎵

This week’s Listening Party will be led by LCC Social Media & Marketing Intern, Alenis! 🎉

When: Thursday, March 20th
Time: 2pm-3pm
Where: Latino Cultural Center-Lecture Center B2

 @uicheritagegarden will also be bringing out their amazing TEA LIBRARY in the LCC Gallery, stop by to make some tea or take some home from our very own gardens! 

Enjoy some jams and chat with friends in the LCC Gallery! We only have TWO listening parties left for the semester so make sure to stop by if you haven’t already! 🎉

Happy Spring Equinox!
——
🐛🔥🎵 What are LCC Listening Parties? 🐛🔥🎵

Our weekly Listening Parties are a part of our intern-led program series where our LCC interns and leaders facilitate a public program in the LCC Gallery. During our Weekly Listening Parties, we come together to build community, share and experience music together, and explore different genres of music.".
