posts.caption_raw:
"Join us for our upcoming 🎥📽️CIVIC CINEMA 🎞️ happening this THURSDAY! 

🌺Jamaica y Tamarindo: Afro Tradition in the Heart of Mexico 🌺 A short film by Ebony Bailey

Presented by the @uiclcc and @bccatuic

In addition to being joined by Chicago cultural worker @dor_doom for a post-screening facilitation and brief presentation on Afro-Mexican identity in Guerrero, we will now be joined by the @uiclibrary's Mobile Pop-Up Library and @sdsatuic! 

📚 The UIC Pop-Up Library will bring a selection of titles related to the themes of the film and UIC students will be able check-out a book during the program! 📚

We hope to see you there! Below is more information on Thursday's program: 

When: Thursday, March 13th, 2025
Where: Latino Cultural Center- Lecture Center B2
Time: 1pm-2pm

“Jamaica y Tamarindo: Afro Tradition in the Heart of Mexico” spotlights the lives of five diverse Afro-Mexicanos as they explore their intersecting identities through their heritages while living in Mexico City. Stay for a brief post-film conversation featuring Diosmery Roque and enjoy fresh aguas frescas!

Film Duration: 20 min. Film is in Spanish with English subtitles. Post-screening conversation will happen in English.

Aguas frescas are first come, first served.

For access needs or questions, email lcc@uic.edu or call 312-996-3095. The LCC is wheelchair accessible. Auto-captions will be available during screening. All audiences are welcome.

This program is co-sponsored by the @uiclibrary & @sdsatuic

Diosmery is a first-generation college graduate with a Bachelor’s degree in Sociology and History from Roosevelt University. She is of Afro-Mexican roots in the state of Guerrero. Where she visits every year in December. Diosmery currently lives in DuPage County, where she continues her journey to decolonize. Her favorite snack is fried Plantains! After the screening, Diosmery will facilitate a conversation about the film and will briefly present on Afro-Mexican identity in Guerrero.
———
The LCC Civic Cinema series shows documentary films to engage different voices on campus and neighboring communities in dialogue about pressing social and environmental issues affecting our lives. 🎞️".

posts.caption_raw:
"❗🧠 Zona Abierta: Mental Health Among Communities of Color 🧠❗

Presented by the UIC Latino Cultural Center 

💡Join us as UIC postdoctoral scholars share their experiences and respective research with the UIC community. Through an intersectional lens, these scholars reveal the complexity and specificity of their contemporary research and how the lives of those in the margins are impacted. 

WHEN: Wednesday, March 19, 2025 | 2-3pm CST 

WHERE: Virtually on Zoom 
RSVP: https://go.uic.edu/ZA319

🧠 Dr. Abhery Das, from the Division of Health Policy & Administration in the School of Public Health, will present on the inequities in social systems and mental health among communities of color. She will discuss two studies which focus on how racialized economic segregation impacts the suicide among Black youth and how hate crimes correspond with greater psychiatric emergencies among Asian Americans. 

❗For access needs or questions, email lcc@uic.edu or call 312-996-3095. Auto-captions enabled on Zoom. ASL Interpretation services may be available by contacting the LCC 5 business days before the program. All audiences are welcome. 

❗Upcoming Presentations: 
Dr. Kumar Ramanathan, Wednesday April 16, 2025 | 2-3pm CST | Department of Political Science

We hope to see you there! Make sure to RSVP via: go.uic.edu/ZA319
------
The LCC Zona Abierta series consist of public programs that highlight the intersection of arts, humanities, science, culture and civic life with presentations by local, national and international artists, scholars and community leaders about pressing social and environmental issues affecting the lives of Latines and Latin Americans, while making connections with other communities.".

WHERE: Virtually on Zoom 
RSVP: https://go.uic.edu/ZA319

🧠 Dr. Abhery Das, from the Division of Health Policy & Administration in the School of Public Health, will present on the inequities in social systems and mental health among communities of color. She will discuss two studies which focus on how racialized economic segregation impacts the suicide among Black youth and how hate crimes correspond with greater psychiatric emergencies among Asian Americans. 

❗For access needs or questions, email lcc@uic.edu or call 312-996-3095. Auto-captions enabled on Zoom. ASL Interpretation services may be available by contacting the LCC 5 business days before the program. All audiences are welcome. 

❗Upcoming Presentations: 
Dr. Kumar Ramanathan, Wednesday April 16, 2025 | 2-3pm CST | Department of Political Science

We hope to see you there! Make sure to RSVP via: go.uic.edu/ZA319
------
The LCC Zona Abierta series consist of public programs that highlight the intersection of arts, humanities, science, culture and civic life with presentations by local, national and international artists, scholars and community leaders about pressing social and environmental issues affecting the lives of Latines and Latin Americans, while making connections with other communities.".
Account: UIC Latino Cultural Center.

posts.caption_raw:
"🎥📽️CIVIC CINEMA - SPRING 2025 🎞️

Presented by the  @uiclcc and @bccatuic 

🌺Jamaica y Tamarindo: Afro Tradition in the Heart of Mexico 🌺 A short film by Ebony Bailey 

When: Thursday, March 13th, 2025
Where: Latino Cultural Center- Lecture Center B2
Time: 1pm-2pm

“Jamaica y Tamarindo: Afro Tradition in the Heart of Mexico” spotlights the lives of five diverse Afro-Mexicanos as they explore their intersecting identities through their heritages while living in Mexico City. Stay for a brief post-film conversation featuring Diosmery Roque and enjoy fresh aguas frescas!

Film Duration: 20 min. Film is in Spanish with English subtitles. Post-screening conversation will happen in English.

Aguas frescas are first come, first served. 

For access needs or questions, email lcc@uic.edu or call 312-996-3095.  The LCC is wheelchair accessible. Auto-captions will be available during screening. All audiences are welcome.

This program is co-sponsored by the @uiclibrary 

Diosmery is a first-generation college graduate with a Bachelor’s degree in Sociology and History from Roosevelt University. She is of Afro-Mexican roots in the state of Guerrero. Where she visits every year in December. Diosmery currently lives in DuPage County, where she continues her journey to decolonize. Her favorite snack is fried Plantains! After the screening, Diosmery will facilitate a conversation about the film and will briefly present on Afro-Mexican identity in Guerrero.

———-
 The LCC Civic Cinema series shows documentary films to engage different voices on campus and neighboring communities in dialogue about pressing social and environmental issues affecting our lives. 🎞️".

posts.caption_raw:
"❗❗❗Zona Abierta ❗❗❗

Biological Causes of Neurodevelopmental Issues: The Role of Environmental Exposures...featuring Dr. Victor Florez-Garcia 🎉

Join us as UIC postdoctoral scholars share their experiences and respective research with the UIC community. Through an intersectional lens, these scholars reveal the complexity and specificity of their contemporary research and how the lives of those in the margins are impacted.

WHEN: Wednesday, March 12, 2025 | 2-3pm CST
WHERE: Virtually on Zoom

❗️RSVP: https://go.uic.edu/B2F312

Dr. Florez-Garcia, from the Division of Epidemiology & Biostatistics, will present previous research on the impact of metal exposures on children's intellectual performance in Mexico. He will also discuss ongoing efforts to assess environmental exposures (POPs and Metals) in the United States. His research employs cutting-edge epidemiological methods to investigate the role of environmental pollutants in developing multiple chronic conditions.

For access needs or questions, email lcc@uic.edu or call 312-996-3095. Auto-captions enabled on Zoom. ASL Interpretation services may be available by contacting the LCC 5 business days before the program. All audiences are welcome.

❗️Upcoming Presentations ❗️
Dr. Azadeh Safaeian, Wednesday March 19, 2025 | 2-3pm CST | Division of Health Policy and Administration

Dr. Kumar Ramanathan, Wednesday April 16, 2025 | 2-3pm CST | Department of Political Science

posts.caption_raw:
"The UIC ArabAmCC was honored to bring back Dabka instructor Mohamed Darawsha. Mohamed first came to our center back in 2018, while he was on tour with Firket Al Wishah, a Dabka dance troupe based in Ramallah, Palestine. 

This past fall of 2024, Mohamed came back to teach Dabka in the U.S, with the collaboration of @uspcn and us at the ArabAmCC we decided to do a workshop in Spring 2025. 

Our first Dabka workshop for Spring 2025 was with @sjpuic for the closing of their Apartheid week. Thank you to @uiclcc for allowing us to use your beautiful space. 

Pictures 1-5 ( February 20th, 2024 workshop) photos by staff member Abdallah Altamimi

Picture 6 
(Mohamed & Firket Al Wishah 2018 with ArabAmCC)".

posts.caption_raw:
"Last Thursday, we had our first ARTivism event of the semester: Artmaking for Love, Liberation, and Revolution ❤️ 

Students enjoyed making heart-shaped seed bombs with @uicheritagegarden, button making, paper flowers and card making while also enjoying snacks and coffee.🌹💌

In addition, students were able to contribute to our ongoing discussion on what love looks like in these times of need, so check out our most recent instagram reel where students shared how they are giving love back to their communities 🎤 

Don’t forget to come and check out our second listening party of the semester next Thursday February 27th from 2-3pm where the @thelatinestudentcoalition will also be tabling 🎶".

posts.caption_raw:
"Earlier this week, us alongside the @arabamcc hosted a Tatreez, Bordado, Embriodery event led by Palestinian Tatreez artist @tatreezjenin ! This program was co-developed with @sjpuic and @mesaatuic with additional support from @uicwlrc and @uicgws. 🧵🇵🇸

This was a collaborative public program between the @uiclcc and @arabamcc where we learned the similarities between Tatreez and Bordado (embroidery) in Mexico and Central America! This educational ARTivism program highlights how our very own UIC Centers for Cultural Understanding and Social Change (CCUSC) work across our diverse populations to help us better understand similar and different cultural practices. 🧵🍉

Students were able to learn about the cultural similarities between embroidery in Palestine and Latin America, while taking home their very own Tatreez projects! Stay tuned for our future events.

Lisa Kurian Phillips of @wbezchicago wrote an article featuring the event. You can read the article at: https://www.wbez.org/education/2025/02/20/uic-cultural-centers-and-race-based-college-programs-across-illinois-threatened-by-latest-trump-guidance

Photos 1 & 2 courtesy of Lisa Kurian Phillips.".

posts.caption_raw:
"Last Wednesday, we hosted our first official event of the Spring semester - our annual LCC Affiliated Student Organizations Open House! 🎉🎉🎉 UIC students came by to connect with 14 student organizations on campus- whether you were looking for a cultural connection, ways to get involved in UIC gardens, get more politically active or join greek life we had all of that in our LCC Gallery! 

Make sure to stay connected with the various student organizations by following their pages: 
@sdsatuic, @dxpdelta, @uicheritagegarden, @latinosunidos_uic, @lpa_uic, @thelatinestudentcoalition @chicagoknights87 @slg_sigma, @mesaatuic, @uprs.at.uic, @sjpuic, @anakbayan_atuic, @latineinlawatuic, @frnatuic

If you missed it, make sure to check out our most recent instagram reel where students shared their favorite things about the LCC! 🎤 We hope to see you at our next event happening this THURSDAY! 

This Thursday (2/13), join us for our upcoming Valentine’s Day event - Artmaking for Love, Liberation, and Revolution from 2pm-4pm at the LCC! We will have supplies to make cards, DIY paper flowers, and heart-shaped seed bombs with the @uicheritagegarden! There will also be an opportunity to contribute to our collective “Love is…” artwork! Make sure to stop by for this drop-in program! 🌹💌".

We will have supplies to make cards, DIY paper flowers, and heart-shaped seed bombs with the @uicheritagegarden! There will also be an opportunity to contribute to our collective “Love is…” artwork! Make sure to stop by for this drop-in program! 🌹💌".
Account: UIC Latino Cultural Center.

posts.caption_raw:
"🇵🇸 🍉 Meet the Artist: Tatreez Jenin! 🇵🇸 🍉 

Get to know the artist for our upcoming Tatreez event, highlighting the connections between Palestine, Mexico and Central America! 

@tatreezjenin is a Palestinian tatreez artist that started Tatreez Jenin in 2024 after recognizing the lack of tatreez resources and education in the diaspora. She is a self-taught tatreezer and is deeply passionate about keeping Palestinian cultures and history alive. For Jenin, a threaded needle and fabric are tools of resistance and empowers members of her community to dive into the deep ocean of Palestinian embroidery. It is how we keep Palestinian voices alive and how she aims to humanize the deep identities of Palestinians. 🧵🪡🇵🇸

This upcoming program kicks off @sjpuic's Apartheid Week. Check out their page for their full spread of events. Viva Palest!na!🇵🇸🇵🇸🇵🇸

This program is a collaboration between the @uiclcc and @arabamcc. Co-developed by @sjpchicago by and @mesaatuic. Co-sponsored @uicwlrc and the @uicgws at UIC.

If you have RSVP'd to this program and you will NO longer be able to attend, email nayala3@uic.edu ASAP to give your spot to someone else! This event is currently at full capacity.".

posts.caption_raw: "We are so excited to announce that some talented members from our band will be performing in the @cbdna_national @intercollegiatemb!  Nearly 1,500 nominated students from 200 college bands in 45 states and Puerto Rico will keep the spirit alive by working together in a virtual marching band show. The video will premiere online on the Intercollegiate Marching Band YouTube channel and at the stadium during halftime of the @CollegeFootballNational Championship game on Jan. 11, 2021! Want to learn more? Check out the official press release at https://tinyurl.com/cbdna-imb-pr and follow @intercollegiatemb today! Finally, make a donation to support this endeavor at fansraise.com/imb.".

"Today we celebrated an amazing class of graduating seniors. This group has made so many contributions to the band, and we want to thank them for everything they have done over the years. Thank them for the early mornings, long days, and late nights. Thank them for sparking new traditions, arranging their own music, fixing broken instruments, inspiring their peers, and the countless hours making the band better. Thank you Alex, Kevyn, Omero, Nathan, Brian, Claudine, Matthew, and Kayla! 🔥 (PC: @samthesaxplaya) .
.
.
.

posts.caption_raw:
uicradio March 1, 2024: "We’re so excited to announce BATTLE OF THE BANDS 2024—reimagined—coming this April! We can’t wait to share more details, but applications for UIC bands and artists to compete for a spot on the SPARK lineup are open now! 🎸⚔️

And for the first time this year, UIC comedians can apply through the same form to enter and compete for the MC role at SPARK! 

Bands must have a minimum of 2 current UIC students to qualify. Solo artists can apply to compete as well!

Who’s excited for the showdown? Comment with #botb2024 if you are ‼️🏵️".

Bands must have a minimum of 2 current UIC students to qualify. Solo artists can apply to compete as well!

Who’s excited for the showdown? Comment with #botb2024 if you are ‼️🏵️".

uicsab August 5, 2025: "👽🤠 Cowboys vs. Aliens — who’s taking over Flames After Dark?

We’re teaming up with RHA for a wild night of FREE pizza (for the first 100 students) , bowling, karaoke, laser tag, billiards, crafts, scavenger hunts & more! 🍕🎤🎯
 🪩 Dance room’s open till 2AM — pull up and show out.

💫 Whether you rockin’ a cowboy hat or a space helmet, we wanna see your best fit. 10PM–2AM — it’s gonna be outta this world.

Flames After Dark is only open to UIC students - no pre-registration is required.
 
🪐💫 Don’t be left in the dust because this event will be out of this world register on campus groups now!🔥

posts.caption_clean:
"ASB Day 5 ✅ Building, learning, and making an impact!
 
🏡 We rolled up our sleeves with Habitat for Humanity, helping build a home for a family in need. Every nail, every beam, and every effort brought us closer to making a tangible difference in someone’s life—an incredibly rewarding experience!
 
🏛️ After a hard day’s work, we explored the Cherokee National History Museum, diving into the rich history, resilience, and cultural heritage of the Cherokee Nation. From centuries-old artifacts to powerful stories of survival and sovereignty, it was an eye-opening journey through time.
 
#UIC #ThisIsUIC #UICSLCE #Lead #Serve #Engage #ASB2025 #HabitatForHumanity #BuildingHope #CherokeeNation #HistoryMatters".

🏛️ After a hard day’s work, we explored the Cherokee National History Museum, diving into the rich history, resilience, and cultural heritage of the Cherokee Nation. From centuries-old artifacts to powerful stories of survival and sovereignty, it was an eye-opening journey through time.
 
 ".
Account: UIC Student Leadership & Civic Engagement.
Topics: this is uic, uicslce, lead, serve, engage, asb2025, habitat for humanity, building hope.

posts.caption_raw:
"ASB Day 4 ✅ A day of service, connection, and cultural learning!

❤️ As part of the Legacy Living elder initiative, we spent the morning giving back—helping load the wood logs for heating and cooking. It was a powerful reminder of the importance of service and intergenerational support.

📖 We also deepened our understanding of Cherokee history and artistry, gaining insight into the rich traditions that continue to shape the Nation today.

🎯 To top it off, we learned and played Chunkey, an ancient Indigenous game that challenged both skill and strategy!

#UIC #ThisIsUIC #UICSLCE #Lead #Serve #Engage #ASB2025 #CherokeeNation #LegacyLiving #CommunityService #CulturalConnections".

posts.caption_clean:
"ASB Day 2 ✅ Another incredible day of learning, preserving, and connecting!
 
🏛️ We had the honor of hearing from Dr. Leslie Hannah, president of Bacone College who led us on an insightful tour of Bacone College, where we explored its rich history and role in Indigenous education.
 
📜 We continued our hands-on work with The United Keetoowah Band of Cherokee Indians, cataloging historical documents.
 
🧺 Plus, we got creative with a hands-on basket weaving session, learning a traditional art form that carries deep cultural significance.
 
#UIC #ThisIsUIC #UICSLCE #Lead #Serve #Engage #ASB2025 #IndigenousHeritage #PreservingHistory #BaconeCollege #CherokeeNation”".

posts.caption_clean:
"ASB Day 1 was filled with history, culture, and hands-on learning!
 
✨ We kicked off with The United Keetoowah Band of Cherokee Indians, gaining insight into their impactful work and engaging in the preservation of Native American art, artifacts, and historical documents. 
 
🖤 Next, we walked the historic streets of Black Wall Street in Tulsa, immersing ourselves in the resilience and legacy of Deep Greenwood. From economic empowerment to the strength of a community that rebuilt after tragedy, this tour was both eye-opening and inspiring.

Lookout for the signed copy of the America’s Black Wall Street by Chief Egunwale Amusan in the UIC Library in the coming weeks.
 
#UIC #ThisIsUIC #UICSLCE #Lead #Serve #Engage #ASB2025 #IndigenousHeritage #BlackWallStreet".

posts.caption_raw:
"Leading by Reading: Banned Book Club reads . . . Parable of the Sower
Thu, Mar 13, 2025
2 PM – 3 PM CDT
Student Center East 2nd Floor Meeting Room 251–Commuter Resource Center, Student Center East
750 S Halsted St 

Join us to collectively read and discuss the graphic novel adaptation of Octavia E. Butler’s novel Parable of the Sower by Damian Duffy and John Jennings. We will spend 40 minutes reading quietly together in a relaxed, laid-back setting with snacks! We will spend the last 20 minutes discussing the text.

Parable of the Sower is set in a post-apocalyptic Earth heavily affected by climate change and social inequality. The novel follows Lauren, who navigates a dystopian future, developing a new philosophy called Earthseed, and leading survivors to build a better future.

Parable of the Sower by Octavia E. Butler has been banned in some schools and prisons because it discusses racism, classism, and climate change. The book is a commentary on what American society could become if it doesn’t change.

Everyone will leave with a copy of the book! Please register on UIC Connect. We will also be emailing you to confirm your attendance a few days before the event, and we will need you to reply to ensure we have a copy of the book for you!".

posts.caption_raw:
"Join us for UIC’s LeaderShape Institute 2025! A collaboration between University of Illinois Chicago, Northern Illinois University, and The LeaderShape Institute.

Institute Dates: May 12-May 16, 2025
Location: Plymouth, Indiana (Transportation from UIC provided)
Preferred Application Deadline: Friday, Apr. 4, 2025

The LeaderShape Institute is a four-day, immersive experience that challenges participants to clarify their values and create their vision for a just, caring, thriving world. Not a typical leadership retreat, The LeaderShape Institute enhances participants’ leadership capacity in service of integrity-driven leadership on their campus, in their community, within their organization, and beyond. Through self-assessment, simulations, intensive small group discussions led by trained facilitators, and more, participants crystalize their leader identity.

For more information and to apply, visit:  slce.uic.edu/leadership/institute".

A collaboration between SLCE & Campus Advocacy Network
Tuesday, Dec. 3, 2024
4:30 PM — 5:30 PM
Innovation Lounge, Commuter Resource Center, Student Center East Second Floor

posts.caption_raw:
"Leading By Reading: Banned Book Club
RSVP on UIC Connect or by scanning the QR code

Fri, Dec 6, 2024
12:30 PM – 2:30 PM
Student Center East 2nd Floor
Student Engagement Conference Room, Commuter Resource Center

Join us to collectively read and discuss the graphic novel MAUS by Art Spiegelman. We will spend one hour reading quietly together in a relaxed, laid back setting with snacks! We will spend the second hour discussing the text. 

MAUS is the story of Vladek Spiegelman, a Jewish survivor of Hitler’s Europe, and his son, a cartoonist coming to terms with his father’s story. One of the most banned books in the country, MAUS is an important book to explore at this time.

“We know that MAUS, a wonderful graphic novel, which really looks at the horrors of the holocaust was a book that recently was banned . . .

I think that we have to understand that anytime there’s a concerted effort to censor books, it’s also about repressing speech, and autonomy, and agency for individuals. I would say let’s not take this lightly. We do need to stand up against this encumberment of freedom of speech, because what we know is that once we begin to see one area of our Bill of Rights taken away, that impacts other areas. So, for us to keep our country one that believes in that everyone has a right to read, and to self-expression—we do need to stand up against censorship. We cannot take this sitting down.”

—Tracie D. Hall, Executive Director of the American Library Association for Library Leadership Podcast

We believe that by reading banned books, we uplift the voices and leadership of so many advocates of change across the country. And by listening and learning from more stories, we ourselves can become better leaders.".

posts.caption_raw: "January 23rd is the last day to add or make changes to 16-week courses, drop a 16-week course without a W on your record, or drop all eligible courses to receive 100% cancellation of tuition and fees. Make sure your schedule is finalized before the deadline! For any questions on adding or dropping courses, make sure to check with your Academic Advisor and contact the Office of the Registrar at registration@uic.edu for any assistance. Please keep in mind that any changes to your schedule during the add/drop period can impact your financial aid, including grant, loan, and scholarship eligibility.".

3. Complete undergraduate degree within 180 credit hours.

posts.caption_raw:
"CHECK OUT OUR UPCOMING EVENTS FOR OCTOBER!🔥

"‼️EVENT ALERT‼️

Presented by Student Employment:

Join this co-facilitated presentation with Studio Institute to learn about the Job Location and Development Program and how to network with employers. Hear from Studio Institute representatives to learn about networking and career development preparation within the field of arts.

Studio Institute is a program intended to provide opportunities for learners of all ages within visual arts education and career development opportunities. They house positions such as summer art opportunities, art instructors, program assistants, and more.

• Virtual event is open to ALL majors and is a great opportunity for major and career exploration!😁

Interested majors Include: Design & Applied Arts, Marketing, Architecture, Industrial & Interior design, Liberal Arts

Registration link is in our bio!💻".

posts.caption_raw:
"Join us as we honor the life of UIC student Ruth E. George with a heartfelt birthday celebration centered on campus safety, connection, and community. Let’s gather in her memory to create a safer UIC for all!

Tuesday, April 8, 2025
1 PM – 3 PM
UIC Quad

🧁 Free cupcakes + coffee
🎉 Interactive activities
🫶 Campus & community orgs

Free & open to the public – All are welcome.
Link in Bio for more Info!

Hosted by @uicaarcc, @uicwlrc, and @dem_psi. 

.

[ID1: Ruth George smiling toward the camera.

ID2: "Together for Ruth: A Birthday Celebration for Campus Safety, Connection, & Community" and event details in pink and purple text, surrounded by balloons, cupcakes, and colorful shapes on a yellow background. At the top is a photo of Ruth George.]

.

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UIC #ThisIsUIC #UICLife #UICEvents #UICFlames #UICBound #SAAM #UICCommunity #CampusSafety #RuthGeorge #UICSAAM #SexualAssaultAwarenessMonth #SexualAssaultAwareness #EndGenderBasedViolence #MeToo".

posts.caption_raw:
"Join us for a powerful night of music, connection, and conversation with artists and community leaders Maggie Arthur (@callingallcrows), Rudy Vecchia-Zeitz (@resiliencechi), Kevante “K.O.” Weakley (@bookclubchi), and Dialect Tre about creating safe & consensual live music spaces—plus, an open mic to uplift your voice! 

Thursday, April 10, 2025
4–6 PM 
605 Student Center East
RSVP at LINK IN BIO!

Want to perform? SIGN UP for the open mic and share your art in a space that centers safety, connection, and creativity.

Co-sponsored by @callingallcrows and @afrouic.

.

[ID: A colorful flyer with a light pink background promotes an event called "Safe Beats, Stronger Communities: A Conversation & Open Mic" in bold orange and magenta text. Silhouettes of musicians playing instruments across the center.]

.

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UICBound #UICLife #ThisIsUIC #UICFlames #UIC #UICEvents #SAAM #SexualAssaultAwarenessMonth #MusicHeals #UICSAAM #SexualAssaultAwareness #EndGenderBasedViolence #MeToo #ChicagoMusic".

posts.caption_raw:
"The Women's Leadership and Resource Center and Campus Advocacy Network will be closed March 24-28, 2025.

If you experience any kind of gender-based or interpersonal violence (such as sexual assault, domestic/dating violence, harassment, or stalking) or need to talk to someone, you can contact a 24-hour hotline anytime to get support from an advocate via phone, text, or chat. More details at link in bio.

We look forward to seeing you when we reopen on March 31.

Wishing you a safe spring break, Flames!

,

[ID1: Pastel-colored flowers, leaves, and branches framing a white square. In the center is dark blue and olive green text that reads,"Spring Break Hours. WLRC and CAN will be closed March 24-28."

ID2: Pastel-colored flowers, leaves, and branches framing a white square. In the center is dark blue and olive green text that reads, "Visit us online for more info & support resources: wlrc.uic.edu •  can.uic.edu. We look forward to seeing you when we reopen on Monday, March 31. Wishing you a safe spring break! ]

.

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UIC #ThisIsUIC #UICLife #UICBound #UICFlames #SpringBreak".

posts.caption_raw:
"Our symposium on the experiences and needs of pregnant and parenting students is coming up this Thursday! Meet the speakers and register to join us!

FROM SURVIVING TO THRIVING:
CULTIVATING POSSIBILITIES FOR PARENTING STUDENTS IN ILLINOIS
Thursday, March 20, 2025
9am – 1pm
UIC SSB & Zoom
Free and open to the public
Register at Link in Bio!

.

[ID1: Two parents wearing commencement robes and mortar boards, playing with their small children, all smiling. Text in the center reads, "from Surviving to Thriving: Cultivating Possibilities for Parenting Students in Illinois." Below that is the date, time, and location of the event and "Meet Our Speakers!"

ID2: "From Surviving to Thriving" in purple text with a green branch and three leaves in place of the second "i" in "Thriving." To its right is "Panel 1: Parenting Students Experiencing Institutions." Below that are photos and names of the Panel 1 speakers.

ID3: "From Surviving to Thriving" in purple text with a green branch and three leaves in place of the second "i" in "Thriving." To its right is "Panel 2: Visions for the Future." Below that are photos and names of the Panel 2 speakers.

ID4: "From Surviving to Thriving" in purple text with a green branch and three leaves in place of the second "i" in "Thriving." To its right is "Keynote Speaker." Below that is a photo of Dr. Sekile Nzinga.

ID5: Two parents wearing commencement robes and mortar boards, playing with their small children, all smiling. Text in the center reads, "from Surviving to Thriving: Cultivating Possibilities for Parenting Students in Illinois." Below that is "Join us! go.uic.edu/spsymp."]

.

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UIC #ThisIsUIC #UICLife #UICEvents #UICFlames #UICBound #StudentParent #StudentParents".

posts.caption_raw:
"Author, activist, and filmmaker Curtis Chin (@curtischin8) is coming to UIC! Join us for 2 events on this special day!

Tuesday, March 4, 2025
✒Memoir Writing Workshop: 12:30–1:45 PM | 215 Taft Hall & Zoom
📖 Book Reading & Signing: 3:00–4:30 PM | Daley Library 1-470 & Zoom

In his memoir, _Everything I Learned, I Learned in a Chinese Restaurant_, Chin shares his journey of growing up Asian in Detroit, coming out to his working-class immigrant family, and navigating the political and cultural landscape of the ‘80s.

Hear Curtis read from his book & grab a signed copy!
Free & open to the public | CART live captioning available

Check out the link in our bio to RSVP!

Hosted by the UIC AANAPISI Initiative and co-sponsored by @uicaasap, @uicgsc, @uicaarcc, @uiclibrary, @uicglas, @invisible2invincible, @restoriedbookshop, and @uicwlrc.

.

[ID: Curtis Chin, wearing a blue hooded sweatshirt and glasses, smiling. Below him is the cover of his book, Everything I Learned, I Learned in a Chinese Restaurant. Around them is text describing the book reading and writing workshop events.]

.

#UICWLRC #UICEvents #UICCAN #UICBound #UICLife #ThisIsUIC #UICFlames #UIC #CurtisChin #AsianAmericanVoices".

posts.caption_raw:
"Black women have long championed self- and community care for survival, resistance, and healing. As Audre Lorde reminds us, self-care "is not self-indulgence—it is self-preservation, and that is an act of political warfare."

This Black History Month, we honor the legacy of women who have redefined care, community, and activism. From rest as resistance to storytelling as liberation, their contributions continue to shape the fight for justice.

How do you practice radical self-care and community care? Drop a comment below!

.

[ID 1: An orange background with a torn-paper collage. Bold black text features an Audre Lorde quote: "Taking care of myself is not self-indulgence, it is self-preservation, and that is an act of political warfare."

ID 2: A black-and-white portrait of @taranajaneen with a purple gradient behind her. Text reads:"Tarana Burke
Founder of the metoomvmt.
Advocates for healing as a form of justice, centering survivors' well-being in activism. Encourages storytelling and community support as self-care."

ID 3:  A black-and-white photo of Audre Lorde speaking into a microphone. Text reads:
"Audre Lorde
Poet, Activist, and Feminist
Taught that self-care is a political act, especially for marginalized people. Practiced self-care through writing, reflection, and community-building."

ID 4: A photo of a silhouette holding a megaphone near a window. Text reads:
"Mariame Kaba
Prison Abolitionist & Organizer
Emphasizes collective care and mutual aid as essential for justice. Promotes restorative justice as an alternative to harm and punishment."

ID 5: A portrait of Tricia Hersey sitting on a couch. Text reads:
Tricia Hersey
Founder of @TheNapMinistry
Advocates for rest as resistance against grind culture. Believes in reclaiming rest, slowing down, and dreaming as radical self-care.

ID 6: A photo of Ericka Huggins speaking into a microphone. Text reads:
"Ericka Huggins
Black Panther & Educator
Introduced meditation and mindfulness in activism. Used spiritual wellness as a tool for resilience and community empowerment."]

.

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UICBound #UICLife #ThisIsUIC #UICFlames #UIC #BlackHistoryMonth #BHM #CommunityCare #RadicalSelfCare #UICBHM".

"Navigating relationships after experiencing violence can be challenging—whether at home, work, school, or online. Join us next Wednesday for a conversation on healing, boundaries, and healthy relationships. Share stories, troubleshoot challenges, and explore what feels right for you!

HEALS: What Now? Relationships, Boundaries, and Consent After Trauma
Wednesday, February 26, 2025
12 - 1 PM
1700 SSB & Zoom
Free lunch for in-person attendees!
RSVP at the Link in Bio!

.

[ID: An illustrated graphic titled "Healthy Relationships and Boundaries After Trauma" features diverse individuals engaging in conversations and interactions in various settings. The image is divided into sections, each highlighting a different aspect of relationships and boundaries:

"Home and Workspace"
"Healthy Workspace Consent"
"School Communication and Consent"
"School Healthy Relationships".]

.

posts.caption_raw:
"Looking for a paid summer internship?

The @uicheritagegarden is now accepting applications for its Summer 2025 Internship Program! This student-led, hands-on opportunity allows undergraduate students to explore environmental, climate, and social justice topics through an intercultural lens.

Positions Available:
🌻 Garden Interns – Research plants, engage in monarch conservation, lead discussions, and more!
📸 Photography & Media Intern – Capture program activities, edit images, and contribute to social media.

DACA & RISE Act eligible students are encouraged to apply! Apps due March 1.

Visit the link in our bio for more information and the application!

.

[ID 1: Two students are working in a garden, one wearing a denim jacket with glasses and the other wearing a black tank top and a blue baseball cap. They are engaged in conversation while tending to plants.

ID 2: A student wearing a pink shirt and yellow shorts is kneeling by a garden, watering plants with a yellow watering can. The garden is lush and green, with tall plants surrounding them.

ID 3: A flyer for the UIC Heritage Garden Summer 2025 Internship with details about the $3,900 paid internship, eligibility for DACA and RISE Act students, and a QR code for applications. A bright sunflower is in the top right corner]

. 

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UIC #ThisIsUIC #UICLife #UICEvents #UICFlames #UICBound #UICHeritageGarden".

posts.caption_raw:
"This Wednesday, celebrate Valentine's Day with HEALS! 💕 

We’ll be writing love letters to ourselves and the survivors in our lives while exploring the power of radical self-love. Let’s celebrate healing and resilience together. Join us!

Wednesday, February 12, 2025
12-1 PM
1700 SSB & Zoom
Free lunch for in-person attendees!
RSVP at Link in Bio!

HEALS: Healing for Engagement, Action, & Leadership by Survivors is a drop-in gathering space for UIC students who have experienced gender-based violence (domestic/dating violence, sexual assault, harassment, stalking, and family abuse) and those who support them. 

.

posts.caption_raw:
"❌RSVP FORM IS NOW CLOSED ❌ 

🧵Tatreez, bordado, embroidery: Stitching connections between Palestine, Mexico and Central America🧵

This program is presented by the @uiclcc and the @arabamcc 

When: Monday, February 17th, 2025
Where: Latino Cultural Center-Lecture Center B2
Time: 5:00pm-7:00pm

❗️❗️RSVP REQUIRED: Registration closed❗️❗️

Join us for a brief presentation and hands-on workshop learning the historical significance of the traditional Palestinian embroidery practice, known as tatreez. Learn to stitch the ‘Star of Bethlehem’ embroidery motif that originated in Palestine and is widely found across Central American and Mexican embroidery. Workshop led by Palestinian artist, @tatreezjenin! 🇵🇸🧵🍉🌍

🧵 All materials provided. Take home your very own tatreez! 

❗️❗️❗️This event is RSVP ONLY- make sure to sign up as there is limited capacity for this program. Food will be provided.❗️❗️❗️UIC students will be prioritized as this is a UIC event.

This program is cosponsored by @uicwlrc and the @uicgws at UIC. 🍉🍉🍉

This program is co-developed by UIC student organizations @sjpuic and @mesaatuic. This program kicks off the first of various program series for the 2025 @sjpuic Apartheid week. 🇵🇸🇵🇸🇵🇸

🎉 We hope to see you there! 🍉🍉🍉

For access needs or questions, email lcc@uic.edu or call 312-996-3095. The LCC is wheelchair accessible.".

posts.caption_raw:
"Before we close out January, we want to shine a light on National Stalking Awareness Month.

Stalking is about control, not the victim’s behavior. Victims shouldn’t have to shrink their lives to stay safe. Instead, let’s hold stalkers accountable and create supportive communities.

Learn the warning signs:
👉 Surveillance
👉 Life invasion
👉 Intimidation
👉 Interference

Ask yourself, how would you help a friend? Supporting victims means listening, believing them, and prioritizing their safety.

Visit the Link in our Bio for more resources, facts, and ways to take action from SPARC (@followuslegally).

.

[ID 1: Text on a navy blue background reads, "IT’S STALKING" and "Surveillance, Life invasion, Intimidation, Interference through sabotage or attack." Icons representing stalking activities, such as cameras, locks, and location pins, line the bottom. The National Stalking Awareness Month logo is at the bottom.

ID 2: A person wearing a hospital gown on a dark blue background featuring medical symbols such as stethoscopes, syringes, and hospital beds in lighter blue. Text reads, "Stalking is a PUBLIC HEALTH ISSUE." The National Stalking Awareness Month logo is displayed below. 

ID 3: A figure stands at a crossroads with directional signs pointing to Home, Work, Grocery Store, Gym, and School. Large text at the top reads, "For many stalking victims, nowhere feels safe." The National Stalking Awareness Month logo is at the bottom.

ID 4: A person is hunched over, carrying the burden of several signs of phrases often told to stalking victims: "You should really get a new phone number," "Don't go out alone," and "Get off social media." On the right, white text reads, "Stalking victims are often advised to make their lives smaller. Help victims balance safety and freedom." The National Stalking Awareness Month logo is at the bottom.]

.

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UICBound #UICLife #ThisIsUIC #UICFlames #UIC #Stalking #StalkingAwareness #StalkingAwarenessMonth #NationalStalkingAwarenessMonth".

Wednesday, January 29, 2025
12-1PM
1700 SSB & Zoom
RSVP at the Link in Bio! 

In this first session of the semester, we’ll discuss where we go for support when we need it and build podmaps of care and community. Free lunch provided! Join us!

.

[ID: "HEALS: Healing for Engagement, Action, & Leadership by Survivors" in white text over a purple background, with flames rising from the bottom center. Below that is white text on a dark purple background describing the HEALS affinity space.]

.

#UICWLRC #UICBound #UICLife #UICCAN #ThisIsUIC #UICFlames #UIC".

posts.caption_raw:
"Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. taught us that greatness is rooted in service:

"Everybody can be great...because anybody can serve. You don’t have to have a college degree to serve. You don’t have to make your subject and verb agree to serve. You only need a heart full of grace, a soul generated by love."

As we reflect on his legacy, let’s remember that change starts with each of us. Whether it’s lending a hand, speaking up for justice, or showing compassion, every effort counts. Together, we can build stronger, kinder communities.

💡 Want to dive deeper? Register for @uicslce's MLK Student Leadership Conference on 1/25, featuring keynote speaker @jahmal_cole of @myblockmyhoodmycity! Details at link in bio.

.

[ID: A vibrant, artistic portrait of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. is centered against a warm-toned background of red and orange hues. A golden frame encases the image, and at the bottom, bold lettering spells 'MLK DAY' in a gradient of gold and navy.]

.

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UIC #ThisIsUIC #UICLife #UICFlames #UICBound #MLKDay #MLK".

posts.caption_raw:
"Kick off the Spring 2025 semester with PERSIST(Σ) and learn about our exciting upcoming events and how you can form part of our community! #uiceiep #uicwiep #persisteuic #uicengineering 

Lunch will be provided. RSVP is required at link in bio! 

All women and nonbinary undergraduate, graduate, and professional students of color are invited.

About PERSIST(Σ):

PERSIST(Σ) aims to create identity-affirming spaces to promote the participation and persistence of women and nonbinary people of color in engineering at UIC. Join the Equity in Engineering Program (EIEP), Women in Engineering Programs (WIEP), and the Women’s Leadership and Resource Center (WLRC) to learn more and be part of this exciting initiative for meaningful change!".

posts.caption_raw:
"Less than 24 hours left before we're all back on campus! Trying to figure out how to spend this snowy day? Check out the shows, music, books, and movies our team recommended for winter break! Whether you’re cozying up with a good read, streaming your favorite series, or relaxing with a new playlist, WLRC and CAN have you covered. Relax, recharge, and embrace the last day of break—you’ve earned it! Can't wait to see you tomorrow!

.

ID 1: A snowy background with large text reading "WINTER BREAK RECOMMENDATIONS" in the center, framed by a cloud. Snowflakes and three poinsettia flowers decorate the scene. Below, a snowman stands in the center with smiling penguins on each side, surrounded by trees and mountains.

ID 2: A snowy background and the heading says "MOVIE PICKS FOR WINTER:". Below is a movie list: Laapataa Ladies, Anora, Wicked, Moana 2, 50 First Dates, and We Live in Time. Below, a person with popcorn and 3D glasses is sitting in a chair and a person is in a chair with a blanket, a laptop, and a warm beverage.

ID 3: A snowy background and the heading says "BINGE WORTHY SHOWS:". Below is a show list: Girls5eva, Broad City, Lakota Nation vs. United States, and Game of Thrones. Below, is a person lounging on a chair, holding a remote, and sitting cross-legged on the floor with a remote and a TV.

ID 4: A snowy background and the heading says "WINTER BREAK PLAYLIST:". Below is a list of music recommendations: Kendrick Lamar's: GNX, Olivia Dean, Katie Gavin: Inconsolable, Bad Bunny: El Club, Rauw Alejandro: Cosa Nuestra, and Chappell Roan. Below, is a snowman wearing headphones and a penguin with headphones.

ID 5: A snowy background and the heading says "BOOKS TO COZY UP WITH:". Below are book recommendations: Social Change Now by Deepa Iyer, Let's Move the Needle by Shannon Downey, Invisible Women by Caroline Criado Perez, and They Can't Kill Us Until They Kill Us by Hanif Abdurraquib. Below, a small penguin is reading a book and a colorful stack of books is illustrated.]
.

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UIC #ThisIsUIC #UICLife #UICFlames #UICBound".

posts.caption_raw:
"Finals are over, winter break is here, and we’ve made it, Flames! 🎉  Enjoy the next few weeks to rest, recharge, and relax. You’ve earned it. ❄️✨

.

[ID 1: A festive winter-themed graphic with a dark blue background decorated with white stars and snowflakes. In the center, there is a green rectangular banner with white text that reads "You did it!" Below, the text says "Happy Winter Break!" in playful fonts. The bottom of the image features snowy hills with evergreen trees in shades of green and blue. 

ID 2: A cartoonish, wide-eyed character looks shocked and confused. The text above reads, “When finals are over, and I’m just standing there like: ‘What now? Do I… relax?"

ID 3: An image of a small dog with its tongue sticking out. The text above reads, “Me pulling up to winter break like: ‘Finals didn’t win, but I definitely lost a few brain cells.’”

ID 4: A man in a flannel shirt stands with an awkward, smug smile. The text above reads, “Me walking into winter break after finals like I definitely didn’t just wing half of those exams.”

ID 5: A small, cartoon chicken character happily flashes peace signs with both hands. The text above reads, “When you realize it’s winter break, and you’re free for the next few weeks.”

ID 6: A young child smirks mischievously, looking pleased with themselves. The text above reads, “When finals are over, and I remember I have no assignments, no alarms, and no responsibilities for a month.”

ID 7:  A cartoon character in a relaxed outfit stands confidently with their hands in their pockets. The text above reads, “When finals had me stressed, but winter break is the chill guy, so now I’m chill too.”

ID 8: A man in a suit sits with a sad expression, holding a holiday-themed mug. The text above reads, “When all you want to do is stay home and watch holiday movies but you have to go to work.”]

.

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UIC #UICBound #ThisIsUIC #UICFlames #UICLife #WinterBreak".

posts.caption_raw:
"WLRC and CAN's office at SSB will be closed December 16, 2024 – January 3, 2025.

Our staff will be working remotely, and you can reach a CAN advocate through December 23:

- Call: (312) 413-8206
- Text: (312) 488-9784
- Email: can-appointment@uic.edu
- Submit an Ask an Advocate form on can.uic.edu.

Outside our regular business hours, you can contact a 24-hour hotline anytime to speak with an advocate via phone, text, or chat. More details at link in bio.

We look forward to seeing you when we reopen on January 6.

Wishing you a warm winter break and a happy new year!

.

[IDs: Each image includes light blue, white, and grey leaves, branches, and stars framing a wide, dark blue square. In the center of each image is light blue text that matches a portion of the text in the caption above.]

.

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UIC #ThisIsUIC #UICLife #UICFlames #UICBound".

posts.caption_clean:
"Finals are here, but don't worry--we've got some laughs and resources to help you get through it! Take advantage of these events to help you stay motivated and supported:

❤️ STUDY FOR FINALS AT WLRC
Need a quiet and supportive space to prepare for finals? Stop by WLRC for a cozy and productive study session! Free snacks & drinks!
Mon 12/9 - Fri 12/13
10am - 4pm
1700 SSB

💙 FREE FINALS CARE PACKAGES
Visit our table at SCE to grab a care package filled with treats, study tips, and affirmations!
Mon 12/9 and Wed 12/11
11am - 2pm
Student Center East Concourse

.

[ID 1: A penguin plush toy with a look of disbelief and the text reads, "Me staring at notes I haven’t looked at since syllabus week."

ID 2: A person is looking frustrated out of a window. The text reads, "Me watching everyone else enjoy life while I’m stuck writing my final paper."

ID 3: A yellow circular object slumped in a chair. The text reads, "Me sitting there wondering if dropping out and becoming a TikTok influencer is still an option."

ID 4: A robot with a blank, neutral face staring ahead: The text reads, "When you realize the final is cumulative and you only studied the last two lectures,"

ID 5: A man with a confused expression: The text reads, "Me after opening any exam and reading the first question."

ID 6: SpongeBob nervously holding a pencil in a classroom: The text reads, "Me trying to hit the word count by adding ‘however’ and ‘therefore’ in every sentence."

ID 7: Two friends are embracing on a couch, and one looks sad and protective. The text reads, "My friends trying to stop me from having a breakdown after the 4th all-nighter."

ID 8: A student studying at a desk with headphones on to promote the "Study for Finals at WLRC" event with bright, calming colors and study-focused imagery.

ID 9: A student holding books with bold text offering encouragement: Highlights the "Finals Care Package" event, with playful colors and shapes listing treats, affirmations, and tips. ]

.

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UIC #ThisIsUIC #UICFlames #UICEvents #UICLife #UICBound #UICEvents #FlamesFinishStrong #FinalsWeek".

ID 4: A robot with a blank, neutral face staring ahead: The text reads, "When you realize the final is cumulative and you only studied the last two lectures,"

ID 5: A man with a confused expression: The text reads, "Me after opening any exam and reading the first question."

posts.caption_raw:
"This Giving Tuesday, join us in supporting UIC's amazing students! 

100% of your gift to the Women's Leadership and Resource Center and Campus Advocacy Network provides

🌞 Tuition and Emergency Assistance to survivors of gender-based violence

🌞 Tuition Assistance to undergraduate women with great financial need (especially LBTQ women, pregnant and parenting students, women of color, women with disabilities, undocumented students, veterans, and students returning after a pause)

so they can continue to pursue their educational, professional, and life dreams. 

❤️ GIVE TODAY: yourgift.uic.edu/GTwlrc (Link in Bio!)

Thank you in advance! We're grateful for our community!

.

[ID: A group of UIC student-parents and their children smiling and posing in front of a shiny backdrop and balloons at "It's a Family Affair," an event hosted by WLRC in May 2024. White text in dark blue bubbles reads "#UICtogether" and "yourgift.uic.edu/GTwlrc." Below that is "Together We Can Change the World" in stylized multicolored text, and the WLRC and CAN logos in red, with a light blue wave between them.]

.

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UIC #ThisIsUIC #UICLife #UICFlames #UICBound #GivingTuesday #UICTogether #TogetherWeCanChangeTheWorld".

posts.caption_raw:
"We're in the final stretch of the semester! Join the students of ART 190: Socially Engaged Art as they host a Cultural Carnival to highlight the seven cultural centers at UIC.

Enjoy free food, live music, face painting, and more! 🍲🎶🖌️

Cultural Carnival
TODAY, Monday, December 2 until 4pm
Student Center East, 3rd Floor, Dearborn AB

Stop by anytime!!

At the event, students will have an access doula booth, earplugs, face masks, and quiet zone to get some studying in, too 📚

Questions? Contact jmoor31@uic.edu.

Thank you to @uic_cada and the students of ART 190 for organizing this event and lifting up the work of the cultural centers! 🎉

.

[ID: Arms with a variety of skin tones stretch to the center, with "Cultural Carnival" in an arch of green '70s letters. Below that are details about the event (same info above).]

.

#UICWLRC #UICCAN #UICCCUSC #UIC #ThisIsUIC #UICLife #UICFlames #UICBound #UICEvents".

posts.caption_clean:
"Who's ready for Thanksgiving break? Hope you’re able to make time to relax, eat some good food, and hang out with loved ones!

Special note: The UIC Pop-up Pantry is open 10am - 4pm today at the Montgomery Ward Gallery in Student Center East! Check out @uicwellnesscenter’s most recent post for details! 

.

[ID 1: A red M&M lies on the ground, clearly distressed, as a yellow M&M crouch to console it. The text above reads: "The mashed potatoes checking on the cranberry sauce after getting passed around 20 times but still untouched."

ID 2: A mischievous cartoon character with exaggerated ears and a sly grin listens intently. The text above reads, "Me listening in from the kitchen as my relatives debate politics over the Thanksgiving table."

ID 3: A stern-looking animated character with dramatic features stares intensely. The text above reads: "When someone says the turkey is 'a little dry,' and it was YOUR turkey."

ID 4: Two prairie dogs, one patting the other on the shoulder, with one saying, "Hol up, let him cook." The text above reads: "When your aunt who usually brings drinks says she’s making the mac and cheese this year."

ID 5: Jimmy Butler squinting at a piece of paper in a basketball setting. The text reads: "Me analyzing my plate trying to decide if I can go back for seconds without looking greedy."

ID 6: A character from a children’s show looks over their shoulder suspiciously in a kitchen. The text above reads: "When someone says 'save me some leftovers,' and you know you’re taking the whole plate home."

ID 7: A distressed SpongeBob in a hallway with the text "please say sike." The text above reads: "When you show up to Thanksgiving dinner and find out there’s no mac and cheese this year."

ID 8: A boy holding a camcorder with an amused expression. The text above reads: "When you’re recording Grandma’s iconic Thanksgiving rant for future generations."

ID 9: A child strapped into a seat looks side-eyed with a concerned expression. The text above reads: "When they tell me to wait for everyone else before I get my plate."]

.

#UICWLRC #UICBound #UICLife #UICCAN #ThisIsUIC #UICFlames #UIC #ThanksgivingBreak".

posts.caption_raw: "The PERSIST(Σ) End of Semester celebration was a blast! 🎉 Huge thanks to all the students who joined us for the winning activity and our bullet journaling intro session—you all rocked it! 🙌 We are SO proud of each and every one of you! 💪 If we don’t catch you before finals, we’re sending all the good vibes your way—you’ve got this! 💫 And don’t forget to join us at EIEP Lounge during finals week for Destress Fest! posts.caption_raw:
"Help us support the Undergraduate Student Government’s (@uicusg)  Food Drive in collaboration with the Wellness Center! 

WLRC is proud to host a donation box for this initiative through Tuesday, November 26. All donations will go directly to the UIC Pop-Up Pantry, which will help provide essential resources for students in need.

Drop-off Location: 1700 SSB
Through Tuesday, November 26

We encourage you to donate non-perishable items like canned goods, cereals, nut butter, and more! Together, we can make a difference and ensure our UIC community feels supported!

.

[ID: A colorful flyer promoting a food drive hosted by the Undergraduate Student Government (USG). The left side has a beige background with bold black text at the top that reads: "Donate to our Food Drive." Below it, the smaller text says: "Make a meaningful impact on our campus community this Thanksgiving season—a celebration of gratitude, hope, and love." Further down, it continues: "Donate the kind of non-perishable foods you’d be grateful to receive—canned goods, chips, nut butters, cereals, or any essentials that would make a difference if they were handed to YOU."

The right side of the flyer features a grid of vibrant illustrations of food items divided into categories. "Breakfast" includes a can, a green bowl of cereal, cashews, and a jar of peanut butter. "Make it yourself" displays a can of tomatoes, dry pasta, sugar, and a bag of flour. "Healthy fats" shows a bottle of olive oil and a jar of coconut oil. "Proteins" includes a can of ham slices, tuna, baked biscuits, chili beans, carrots, pork and beans, and Vienna sausages.

At the bottom left corner, a red circular USG logo reads: "Undergraduate Student Government." A playful note below says: "psst: the center with most donations gets a special prize."]

.

 #UICWLRC #UICCAN #UIC #ThisIsUIC #UICLife #UICFlames #UICBound".

posts.caption_raw:
"Last chance to apply! 

UIC Undergraduates: Apply for the Spring 2025 Diversity Community Engagement Program (DCEP) Internship!

WLRC is looking for interns who want to help us develop programs and events to
👉 support pregnant and parenting students, and/or 
👉 raise awareness of sexual assault and gender-based violence prevention.

More info:

⭐️ Apply to work with UIC's Office of Diversity or cultural centers
⭐️ All UIC undergraduate students are eligible
⭐️ Federal Work Study and DACA students are encouraged to apply
⭐️ 10 hours/week
⭐️ Receive compensation and/or course credit

Applications due by 9am Monday, November 25, 2024.

Details at go.uic.edu/DCEP (LINK IN BIO!).

.

[ID1: White text on top of a collage of photos of past WLRC events that has been washed in purple-blue reads: "WE'RE HIRING! Spring 2025 DCEP Internship with the UIC CCUSC & Office of Diversity. Apply by November 25!

Applications due Mon, Nov 25, 2024 by 9am CST." 3 smiling DCEP interns are in the bottom right corner.]

.

posts.caption_raw:
"On October 31, we were fortunate to host Stephanie Skora (@stephanieskora) for a talk on Civic Engagement for Social Justice. Stephanie energized us to consider what is at stake in our communities, both during election season and at all times, and how we can take action in ways that align with our morals. 

Many thanks to our co-facilitators and co-sponsors, including @jsc_uic, @sdsatuic, @socialjustice_uic, @uicgsc, @uicsoc, @uicglas, @uicgws, @irrppuic, @uicclj, and @uicslce.

.

[ID1: Stephanie Skora speaking at a podium. 

ID2: A member of the UIC Jewish Student Collective speaking at a podium and introducing Stephanie Skora. To their right is a screen with captions. 

ID3: Stephanie Skora posing with members of the Jewish Student Collective. 

ID4: Stephanie Skora posing with members of the Jewish Student Collective and staff of the Women’s Leadership and Resource Center.]

.

#UICWLRC #UICBound #UICLife #UICCAN #ThisIsUIC #UICFlames #UIC".

posts.caption_raw:
"November 20th marks Transgender Day of Remembrance, and we’re honoring and uplifting the voices of our transgender community here at UIC and beyond. TDOR is a time to remember those lost to anti-trans violence and to recommit ourselves to creating safer, more inclusive spaces. 

Check out the Gender and Sexuality Center (@uicgsc) and UIC Law Pro Bono Litigation Clinic (@uic_law) event today. Visit the link in our bio for all the details!

Name & Gender Marker Change Information Session
Wednesday, November 20 2024
12:00–1:30 PM CST
Virtual on Zoom 
Learn how to legally change your name and correct your gender marker in Illinois with guidance from UIC Law’s Pro Bono Litigation Clinic and Gender & Sexuality Center.

.

[ID 1: A soft gradient background with a focus on three lit candles. Text reads, “Remembering Transgender Lives with Love” and “Today is Transgender Day of Remembrance” in bold white letters. At the bottom are the logos for UIC Women’s Leadership and Resource Center and Campus Advocacy Network.

ID 2: A background featuring a transgender pride flag waving softly. The text reads, “Transgender Day of Remembrance (TDOR),” followed by an explanation: “What is it? Transgender Day of Remembrance is an annual observance on November 20th to honor the memory of transgender people whose lives were lost due to anti-transgender violence. Why It Matters: - Raises awareness of violence faced by transgender individuals. - Encourages solidarity and advocacy for trans rights.”

ID 3: A colorful design with a blue and pink background. The text reads, “Name & Gender Marker Change Information Session” followed by the event date, time, and RSVP link. The description below reads, “In recognition of Transgender Day of Remembrance, the Pro Bono Litigation Clinic at UIC Law will go over the process for legally changing your name in Illinois and correcting your gender marker on identification documents.” At the bottom are the logos for the UIC Law Pro Bono Litigation Clinic and Gender and Sexuality Center.]

.

#uiccan #uicwlrc #uiclife #uicevents #uicbound #uicflames #transdayofremembrance #transrightsarehumanrights".

posts.caption_raw: uicwritingcenter March 28, 2024: "While you're planning your schedule for next semester, consider taking our tutor training courses, ENGL 282 and ENGL 482! 📝 Tutors spend one semester training and attending seminar and are then eligible to apply to be paid staff or volunteer tutors! No prior tutoring experience is required. 🤓 This is a great way to expand your own writing skills while also helping your peers in an anti-oppressive environment. DM us or stop by the WC with any questions you may have! 🪴☕️".

posts.caption_raw:
"We’ve been busy this summer at the Writing Center!  First off, we got the chance to try some serviceberries from the trees outside! 

Next, we created pamphlets from career center resources and resume/cover letter tips. We have several different handouts scattered across the Center’s physical space!

We will be closed next Monday (June 19) for Juneteenth and we reopen on Tuesday. Check online for availability! Now is a great time to work on professional development (resumes, cover letters) and creative writing!".

NO GAME THIS WEEK!".

posts.caption_raw: "rugby is back! we will be meeting at 5-6:30 pm on Tuesdays and Thursdays at OFC west! (outdoor field complex). worried we’ll be all up in each other’s business like tackling and scrumming? worry no more! these practices will be NO CONTACT! We are taking necessary precautions to make sure everyone will feel and be safe. We hope to see you there!🏉❤️💙".
